origination.label: Creator
origination.corpname: Princeton University. Library. Department of Rare Books and Special Collections

did.abstract: This artificial collection consists of one thousand original drawings, including a significant number by Charles Lewis Bartholomew, Otho Cushing, Homer C. Davenport, John Tinney McCutcheon, and Frank Arthur Nankivell.
did.physloc: FOR DIGITIZED CONTENT: Cartoons in this collection have been digitized and may be viewed or downloaded through this finding aid. To view each one, navigate to the individual item within this finding aid.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: For preservation reasons, original analog and digital media may not be read or played back in the reading room. Users may visually inspect physical media but may not remove it from its enclosure. All analog audiovisual media must be digitized to preservation-quality standards prior to use. Audiovisual digitization requests are processed by an approved third-party vendor. Please note, the transfer time required can be as little as several weeks to as long as several months and there may be financial costs associated with the process. Requests should be directed through the Ask Us Form.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This artificial collection consists of one thousand original drawings, including a significant number by Charles Lewis Bartholomew, Otho Cushing, Homer C. Davenport, John Tinney McCutcheon, and Frank Arthur Nankivell. Other artists that are well represented include Louis Glackens, Harold Imbrie, Udo J. Keppler, Norman Ritchie, and Fred O. Seibel.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Collection is open for research use.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Single copies may be made for research purposes. To cite or publish quotations that fall within Fair Use, as defined under U. S. Copyright Law, no permission is required. For instances beyond Fair Use, it is the responsibility of the researcher to determine whether any permissions related to copyright, privacy, publicity, or any other rights are necessary for their intended use of the Library's materials, and to obtain all required permissions from any existing rights holders, if they have not already done so. Princeton University Library's Special Collections does not charge any permission or use fees for the publication of images of materials from our collections, nor does it require researchers to obtain its permission for said use. The department does request that its collections be properly cited and images credited. More detailed information can be found on the Copyright, Credit and Citations Guidelines page on our website. If you have any questions, please feel free to contact us through the Ask Us! form.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Material in this collection is assembled from a number of different accessions throughout the years. Nineteen Derso and Kelen cartoons were added in summer 2012, accession number ML.2012.027. A 1931 Otto Cushing cartoon was added in summer 2014, accession number ML.2013.031. A World War II era comic strip produced by the Congress of Industrial Organizations (CIO) was added in 2016, accession number ML.1000.007.

appraisal.head: Appraisal
appraisal.p: This is an artificial collection. No materials have been separated.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: FOR DIGITIZED CONTENT: Cartoons in this collection have been digitized and may be viewed or downloaded through this finding aid. To view each one, navigate to the individual item within this finding aid.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Additional cartoons by many of these artists are located in the Graphic Arts Collection at the Firestone Library, Princeton University. These cartoons can be located by searching the Visuals Database.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Identification of specific item; Date (if known); Political Cartoon Collection, Box and Folder Number; Public Policy Papers, Special Collections, Princeton University Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This is an unprocessed collection. Contents list updated by Adriane Hanson with the assistance of Jeremy Russell in 2010. Derso and Kelen cartoons in June 2012 accession were added to existing cartoons in Box 13 in 2012. A 1931 Otto Cushing cartoon was added to Box 10 in 2014. A World War II era comic strip produced by the Congress of Industrial Organizations (CIO) was added to Box 29 in 2016.

scopecontent.p: GOP presidential candidate Wendell Wilkie (1940) hiding behind tree with huge gun of his "War on Bossism" only to be surprised by Tammany Tiger from behind.

scopecontent.p: John L. Lewis, president of the C.I.O, trying to decide which presidential candidate to endorse. He eventually staked his position on Willkie and resigned when FDR won.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Stretcher bearers bring in "that Republican campaigner" (Wendell Willkie) who has "got his foot in his mouth again."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: GOP elephant spanking Col. Knox and Col. Stimson for accepting New Deal cabinet posts saying, "Boy, this hurts me worse'n does you!"

scopecontent.p: Mussolini paints out list of War Costs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: F.D.R., Hull, and Garner dance around a May Pole and a figure labeled "Somoza."

did.unittitle: "Captain Cleveland's Great Scheme to Save the Sinking Ship of State"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A haggard Cleveland desperately pumps his "Bond Scheme" in an effort to save the Ship of State which is being swamped by waves of interest and expenses. A hopeless and drenched Uncle Sam looks on.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Spain Premier Sagasta and U.S. President McKinley both sign a protocol. Birds and plants surround an abandoned cannon.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President McKinley tries to hold a large gun labeled "Cuban Policy" while Uncle Sam holds a clock.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gresham and President Cleveland sit at a table with a map of Honduras and Nicaragua. An unidentified third figure stands behind them.

did.unittitle: "Secretary of the Interior - Some Inside Facts"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Secretary of the Interior Fisher bursting through a map of Alaska with a paper labeled "Facts."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Seated Republican elephant with pipe in a trunk watching the "Trusts" monopoly pulling a wishbone with "Teddy."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Corn, Wheat, and Oats in human shapes fly over a field in hot air balloons. Another balloon labeled "hot air" and a fuel tank labeled "annual crop scare" are on the ground below.

did.unittitle: "A Bird in the Hand Might Stir up Several in the Bush"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man labeled "Italy" tries to push a bird labeled "Turkey" out of a fenced area into the woods. 5 unidentified men watch from outside of the fence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson sailing in a ship labeled "The Ark of Peace." A dove flies back to him with a branch labeled "Peace Talk."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Woodrow Wilson in a Princeton uniform chasing a figure labeled "Presidential Nomination" around a track. The Democrat Donkey cheers him on and the Republican elephant watches.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Theodore Roosevelt sits on the ground after being bucked by a horse labeled "North Dakota." Roosevelt says "The critter never acted like that before."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Theodore Roosevelt as a giant, tied to the ground outside the White House. Small figures labeled Senate and House run as he begins to break free of the ropes. The ropes are labeled Lumber, Coal, Hides, Iron Ore, Cotton and Wool Schedules, Zinc, (Heavy Tax on the People).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Armored knight with a shield "U.S. Steel Trust" prepares to fight Uncle Same with a sword "U.S. Steel The Law."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Santa Claus flying in his sleigh over the earth, where several countries fire missiles at him. He says "What's the matter with the old world? Don't they know it's Christmas?"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Caption: "Taft. So much more can be accomplished by kindness than by brutality." Taft as a farmer tending to a contented cow labeled "The Hughes Boom." Hughes stands behind, scratching his head.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Governor Hammond signs the "County Option in the State of Minnesota."

did.unittitle: "Never mind, old man, I may be savin' you an awful tummy ache."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Democrat donkey eating a watermelon labeled "Currency Legislation" while the Republican elephant looks on in dismay.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two races. In the first, American revolutionary soldiers chase a British soldier on a road from Concord to Lexington. In the second, a British runner struggles, out of breath, to catch up to an American on a road to London.

did.unittitle: "I'll Just Boss this Job Myself, For a Spell"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam seated on the Congress building as "Senate Boss" Aldrich and "House Boss" Cannon leave.

did.unittitle: "Will he let down the hungry donkey"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hungry Democrat donkey approaches fence of Civil Service Rules being put up by Woodrow Wilson. The fence protects a field of grain labeled "Government Offices."

did.unittitle: Bryan: "I Wonder if My Eyesight is Failing?'

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bryan reading a newspaper with the poll of Congress for President in 1912. Bryan is not on the list.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Figures representing the United States and Germany shake their fists at each other and carry baskets of eggs labeled "trade." Dogs with the label U.S. Tariff and German Tariff have wrapped their leashes around the legs of each figure.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson puts a book on a shelf titled "History of the American People Vol. I" covering the first year of his presidency: a new tariff, new currency law, arbitration treaties, Panama Canal completed, Alaska R.R. projected. On the desk behind him are materials for Vol. II: foreign relations, Panama government, trust legislation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson as school master rings a bell to call Congress (the school house) to an extra session. The school boys are Democrat, Republican, Progressive, and New Member.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A moose on crutches walks down a road next to a sign "The Tall Timber." A man named Frost asks "What's the matter Mr. Moose?" The moose replies "I got kicked by a mule."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Baseball. President Woodrow Wilson pitches to the G.O.P. Republican elephant. Uncle Sam is the umpire and the rest of the team is all labeled "issue."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson holds two crying babies, one the Senate crying "No!" and the other the House crying "Yes." A bottle of Wilson's Soothing Syrup sits on the table.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man outside of Congress holds a scroll reading "Ship Bill Must be Passed W.W." and the severed tail of the Democrat donkey. The tail-less donkey looks back at the man.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Woodrow Wilson as a boy sweeps the snow off a walk labeled "Clean Politics." Bryan tells him "Good Work, My Boy! You May Be President Some Day" as Harmon (as a boy with a sled) looks on.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The White House is full of packages, with a sign "No Entrance for Ordinary Business until After the Wedding."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson, with his bag packed for his Western trip, stands in front of a figure representing Congress. Wilson points to a giant standing behind him, with boots labeled U.S.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson, wearing a graduation cap labeled "1st Term" and with a hat labeled "Second Term" behind his back. A figure labeled "Aunty Democracy" is handing him a top hat labeled "Single Term"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Four frames: Jefferson, Wilson, the Democrat Donkey, and an unidentified man. The first 3 are framed with U.S. Postage 1 cent. The last is framed with scrolls reading "Lecture."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Figure representing Cuban Insurrectionist runs towards a fence with a machete in each hand. Uncle Sam stands behind the fence saying "Look here you can't act like that - you're no presidential candidate, you know!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam has a new ear taped to his head labeled Panama Canal Strip. A figure representing Columbia walks away with a bandage over his missing ear.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A dog labeled "Mexico" eats from the hand of President Woodrow Wilson.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson holding out a spoon of peace plan pills to Huerta, who does not want to take them. Figures representing South America, Japan, England, and France look on smiling.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson in a dress brings a bottle of Wilson's Soothing Syrup for Infant Republics to a crying baby representing Haiti. A happy child representing Mexico is in the background.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two small figures fighting in Mexico are about to get stepped on by a giant boot representing the United States. Two figures in Europe cheer on the giant.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson, with a paper under his arm labeled "Public Business" steps over a building labeled "Ancient Precent" on his way to an Extra Session of Congress. Buildings "Time Honored Tradition," "Custom," and "Old Methods" stand between him and Congress.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Woodrow Wilson drops a suitcase labeled with a Princeton, N.J. address as he is greeted by a woman representing the Democratic Presidential Nomination.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An elderly Uncle Sam whittles while arms labeled "War Loans" stretch out towards him across the ocean from under a black cloud. The shavings from his whittling form the words "Watchful Waiting."

did.unittitle: "The President Satisfied -- They've Doubled the Infantry"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson holds his first grandchild and is being handed Eleanore Axson Sayre, his second grandchild.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Aunty Democracy is trying to make a cake in a bowl labeled "Presidential Nomination." There are several bottles of ingredients with potential nominees. She says "This is where I usually make a mess of it - I suppose now I'll make some mistake as usual."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Champ Clark's Hound Dog howls at the moon, which has a man's face. A man from the House of Representatives brings the dog a dish.

did.unittitle: "And He Slid - Willie Taft 'If Anybody Wants This Sled He'll Have to Come and Take it.'"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A large Taft sits on a sled labeled "The Presidency" on a path labeled 1912. Much smaller Woodrow Wilson and Harmon look on.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson walks with the Democratic Administration donkey and an unidentified gentleman next to a wheat field labeled "Prosperity, 930,000,000 Bushel Wheat Crop"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson reads a book labeled "Tariff, Currency" by the light of a candle labeled "Senate Majority" that burns low in a holder labeled "Extra Session"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson, holding a bill labeled "Tariff Measure" prepares to cross a canyon bridged by a man labeled "Democratic Majority of One in Senate"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Woodrow Wilson looks down at a little girl representing Minnesota, holding a book labeled "Primaries," and asks "Whose Little Girl are You?"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson sits with a child representing the Filipino on his knee and promises that "eight years school in democracy should make you ready for independence."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Woodrow Wilson, his arm in a sling, greets a long line of people and has them shake a wooden arm instead of his own while campaigning for the presidency.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Figures representing the House of Representatives and the U.S. Senate hold the hands of President Woodrow Wilson in front of an American flag.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wilson, Folk, and Harmon as donkeys in the Democratic Presidential Pasture. Champ Clark as a donkey is outside of the pasture eating a thistle labeled "speakership."

did.unittitle: "He Likes Champ Because he's a Regular-Democrat"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Champ Clark as a donkey being brushed by a man representing a Democratic Politician. Wilson and Harmon, as donkeys, look on concerned from behind a fence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A fat man representing the Democratic Congress reads a bulletin that President Wilson is getting stronger and has a frightened vision of Wilson on one end of a saw, expecting him to take the other. The log to be sawed is "New Legislative Program"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Woodrow Wilson sits on a stage, reading from a book, to an audience representing many nations. A stack of other books he will read include Common Sense, National Ethics, Morality, and Fairness.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three frames. 1: Theodore Roosevelt promises if elected to call an extra session of Congress to enact laws of human perfection. A figure representing voters is concerned. 2: Woodrow Wilson promises if elected to call an extra session of Congress to revise the Tariff. The voter is concerned. 3: President Taft promises if re-elected that he will eat more pie. The voter smiles and winks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Woodrow Wilson tells Murphy that there will be no political bosses allowed in this country. Wilson holds a sign behind his back reading "Huerta (the dictator) Must Go!). A figure representing the U.S. Congress laughs in the background.

did.unittitle: "Still Able to Land 'Em in New Jersey"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Woodrow Wilson as a fisherman, having caught a large fish labeled "Jury Reform" in New Jersey.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Woodrow Wilson, Harmon, Champ Clark, and Folk line up before William Bryan asking for a chance to fly a donkey with "Progressive Democracy" wings at the Democratic Convention.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson enters a messy office with a new broom labeled "Walter L. Fisher New Secretary Interior Department." Issues that need cleaning up include Cunningham claims, forest lands, California oil land, mineral lands, conservation, water power sites, and Alaska.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Woodrow Wilson holds a globe on his back, with Democrat Party concerns in place of the continents. North America is Democratic Congress, the Caribbean is Legislation, South America is Coming Congressional Campaign, Europe and Asia are the Democratic Party, and Africa is the Democratic Platform.

did.unittitle: "Mr. Bryan Fails to Discover the Pole."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bryan explores the top of the "World of Politics" but fails to notice Woodrow Wilson, the north pole.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wilson is forced onto the "Single Term Plank" of the ship "Democracy" by the Democrat donkey.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Woodrow Wilson is being given a donut by a woman representing New Jersey. Figures of two other candidates are chased away in the background.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Woodrow Wilson tries to mow the lawn as a stout man with a cigar gives advice.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson, as a circus animal trainer. He directs the Democratic Party donkey, ridden by the Currency Bill, to jump over the U.S. Senate Minority elephant.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Governor of New Jersey Woodrow Wilson throws a figure representing New Jersey Trusts to the ground. A figure representing National Trusts looks at the fight, concerned.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A postcard from Mussolini (in Albania) to Hitler (in Germany): "Dear Addie - Having a terrible time. Wish you were here. Mussie"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Simulated stained glass window depicting history of Irish in America. Saint Patrick is centerpiece.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson forcing a boy representing Congress to keep sawing at wood labeled "Trust Legislation."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Price Administrator Henderson tries to hold down a floating pig labeled "Inflation Threat"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A giant octopus labeled "pressure groups" sits on the top of the capitol, smoking a cigar. Its arms are labeled intimidation, threats, treasury raids, subsidy demands, and log rolling.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A giant snake sits on top of Singapore.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A tank with a large claw labeled the "Nazi War Machine" destroys several buildings and a crowd of people.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A gorilla labeled "Jungle Law" sits in a judge's seat for the case "Civilization vs. Barbarism." There is a Nazi swastika in the background.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A caricature of a Japanese soldier drinks from a bottle of "Victory Hootch" with three bottles under his other arm, a skull visible on one bottle.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A worried Uncle Sam. In the furrows on his forehead are the words "strikes, huge tax burdens, labor racketeering, subversive elements, inflation threat, defense snags, and political swiping."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A donkey struggles under a heavy load of military equipment, ridden by a Japanese soldier, along a road labeled "aggression". The soldier holds out a stick with corn (labeled "peace gestures") to lead the donkey on.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: France, represented by the figure of a woman, lies dying with a broken sword in her hand, crushed by a Nazi swastika.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Soldiers fire on a giant Nazi swastika.

scopecontent.p: Explosion of "Jolly Roger" over the Bryan letter. "I don't know where I am going, but I am on my way."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A crowd of boys is sent from the "Charitable Orphan Asylum" by an angel promising a business education to the Fostoria Glassworks. A fat man with a top hat looks on and grins.

scopecontent.p: Thomas Dewey and Robert Taft, candidates for the Republican presidential nomination (1940), commenting on inability of the GOP nominee, Wendell Willkie, to keep up with "Third Term" donkey of FDR in presidential race.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam leading donkey down "The Way of Democracy" balancing "Alert Opposition" and the "Party in Power" on the donkey's back.

scopecontent.p: A Nazi swastika pulls a bear representing Russia by a chain around the bear's neck labeled "Nazi Diplomacy." The bear pulls against the chain.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hitler and Mussolini removing the Polar Axis from the globe and replacing it with a larger "Rome-Berlin" axis that breaks the earth. Stalin watches, puzzled.

did.unittitle: "The crack of the "gat"- and the crack of the bat."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A figure representing Europe flinches at the sound of a gun, while a figure representing the U.S.A. enjoys a game of baseball.

scopecontent.p: Two "jack-in-the-boxes." A man sits on one, Irish Home Rule, to prevent it from opening. The other, Boer Home Rule, springs open and carries a rifle and a sword.

scopecontent.p: Drunken man labeled "Congress" pledging not to touch bottle labeled "More Spending Recommendations," "Taxpayer" crosses his fingers in the background.
