did.unittitle: "President Wilson's Announcement of U.S. War Aims"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Angry man in military uniform jumps on an announcement of President Wilson's U.S. war aims.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink; crayon and white tempera as corrections all on a cloth-backed paper, 27 1/2 x 13 1/2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three panels showing the same man before Congressional Investigation, State Legislature Investigation, and Municipal Investigation.

physdesc.physfacet: Crayon and ink, with white tempera as corrections on tissue, 24 x 18 3/4

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "National Game of Politics" played by father, son, uncle, husband, and brother on the form of baseball and umpired by a female.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Figure representing the German High Command walks down a ladder on a ship named the S.S. Blitzkrieg. Hitler watches from an upper deck and a crewman thumbs his nose from inside, looking out a portal.

did.unittitle: "Long, long trails a-winding... long, long nights of waiting."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Empty ocean labeled "Pacific Sea Lanes."

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon and mylar overlay, 15 x 20

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The GOP and Dems. at a standoff with Hitler in between saying, "Let's you and him fight."

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with mylar overlay, 15 x 20

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hitler, Mussolini, and Hirohito with their ears to the ground saying, "We're Not Interested in the U.S. Election."

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with mylar overlay, 15 x 20

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The "Third Term Tradition" leaving "The American Scene" as FDR wins a fourth term.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with mylar overlay, 20 x 15

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Football game with FDR running with "U.S. Foreign Policy" ball as GOP, Wendell Willkie, and U.S. Voters sit on bench with Voters saying, "Why Should I Have Taken Him Out. He's Doing All Right."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A grim reaper, his scythe reading "staggering Jap losses" flies over marching Japanese troops. The front lines are being killed.

did.unittitle: "'Tis Annoying After Once Having It All 'Sewed Up'",

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: GOP elephant attempting to find the right thread (candidate) to sew up 1928 Presidential Election again, after Calvin Coolidge announced he was not running.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink and black crayon on pebbleboard, 12 1/2 x 17 1/2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wendell Willkie (1940 Republican Presidential candidate) chopping legs out from under the wooden elephant of "Partisan Leadership" with the ax of "Americanism."

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink and black crayon on pebbleboard, 12 1/2 x 17 1/2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam sitting down for a drink with Wendell Willkie (1940 Republican Presidential Candidate) on his return from campaigning.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink and black crayon on pebbleboard, 12 1/2 x 17 1/2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wendell Willkie's (1940 Republican Presidential Candidate) face breaking through front page of newspaper saying, "I accept."

did.unittitle: "Don't Play Around With Him Too Long!",

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink and black crayon on pebbleboard, 11 x 16

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam holding bear cub labeled "Rising Prices" in inset, main drawing shows Uncle Sam being mauled by full-grown bear of "Inflation."

odd.head: General
odd.p: Star Telegram; Fort Worth, Texas, October 1942.

did.unittitle: "The Perfect Dr. Jekyl and Mr. Hyde!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 figures representing Japan. One apologizes to Uncle Sam and tells him war with the U.S. is unthinkable. The other stabs Uncle Sam in the back.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson leads a blindfolded Uncle Sam out into a body of water labeled "Internationalism."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson and Secretary Bryan in robes in the desert. Bryan leads the donkey of the Democratic Party. They stand in front of an arch reading "Unjust Criticism."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dove of Peace lies bleeding with a bayonet through its heart on European soil.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson as a farmer plowing furrows in front of his house to stop a ranging wild fire. The house is the U.S., the plow is National Defense, the horse is Congress, the furrow is Greater Army and Navy, and the fire is war.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink on pebbleboard, 13 x 16 1/2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Fireworks Manufacturer" with arms full of goods labeled "July 4th Accidents" and "Ohio Legislature" with "Bill to Outlaw Fireworks Sale After August 1st" saying, "After August 1st I'll have to Protect the Children."

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink; black crayon, and white tempera corrections on pebbleboard, 14 x 11

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Dictatorship" entering "U.S. Constitutional Government Stronghold" through door of "Third Term." Refers to FDR's running for a third term in 1940.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink; black crayon, and white tempera corrections on pebbleboard, 14 x 11

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "New Deal Big Gun" aimed at figure of business and private initiative.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink; black crayon, and white tempera corrections on pebbleboard.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Caricature of 1940 Democratic Presidential Convention as hundreds of FDR's nominating himself.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink; black crayon, and white tempera corrections on pebbleboard, 14 x 11

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Throne for "King Franklin" (FDR) with crown of third term suspended on rope of Chicago Convention (site of 1940 Democratic Presidential Convention).

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink; black crayon, and white tempera corrections on pebbleboard, 14 x 11

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gladiator figure of "The Old War Emergency Issue" sitting outside "Offices of the New Deal" saying, "Sure -- I'll just hang around 'til election's over -- just in case you need me."

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink; black crayon, and white tempera corrections on pebbleboard, 14 x 11

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gravestone reading, "R.I.P. '40 The Genuine Democratic Party Chocked Off by New Dealers" with Chicago (site of 1940 Democratic National Convention) in background.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A large arm labeled "drastic law" with a sword in hand comes down upon two figures labeled "tire bootlegger" and "tire thief."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A bald eagle, labeled "America's Full Air Might" struggles to fly while each foot is tied to the ground by "half-way measures."

did.unittitle: "Beset Abroad by Japs - at Home, by Yaps!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A U.S. soldier representing U.S. defense efforts looks towards Japan, as little figures of Americans at his heels complain, sow disunity, and spread false rumors.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Large hand of "Presidential Control" reaches out and grabs two small figures of "U.S. Business."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hitler puts up wallpaper with Nazi swastikas labeled "World Coverage." He is handed a roll of wallpaper of Japanese victories by the Japanese emperor. Mussolini lies, beat up, in a glue bucket.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: German-American adding "The American People" to the list of those deserving God's Punishment after reading paper with headline of "Wilson Re-elected Without the German Vote."

physdesc.physfacet: Blue pencil, black crayon on pebbleboard, 20 x 16

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Figures throwing "dirt, mud, slander," etc. into "Political Campaign Headquarters."

physdesc.physfacet: Blue pencil and ink on pebbleboard, 22 1/4 x 19 3/4

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two women talking over a fence, one saying, "Between you, me, and the gatepost, I understand the Army's going to..." Meanwhile, an Army man sneaks up over the hill with a huge listening horn.

odd.head: General
odd.p: On back: "To Princeton University from the Newark News." Crawford worked for many years at the News.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A knight in full armor rides a horse. The figure is W.W. (Woodrow Wilson) and his lance is Democracy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A Japanese soldier digs in his heels, but also pulls himself forward by a ring in his nose, towards Singapore and the Dutch East Indies. The caption reads "From unimpeachable sources in the Far East it is learned that Japan is straining against bitter odds to maintain peace and harmony with the U.S."

physdesc.physfacet: Blue pencil and ink on pebbleboard, 22 x 19 1/2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A Japanese soldier knocks pieces labeled Singapore, Cavite, Burma, and Pearl Harbor from base of statue of Democracy. Refers to Japan's early successes in World War II.

odd.head: General
odd.p: On back: "To Princeton University from the Newark News." Crawford worked for many years with the News.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson as a cowboy gets ready to ride a bucking horse, Congress. Former president Taft sits on the ground having been thrown by the horse, with a letter inviting him to come to Yale.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A woman, holding a paper reading "Legion of Atonement" and with a sash reading "Hope" encourages prisoners to escape through a hole in the wall. Outside of the hole are American troops in formation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A large figure of Uncle Sam looks down at a small figure of Uncle Joe.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two forms of gambling; on left the stock market, on the right a casino.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Huerta, Villa, and Carrans each reach out to a woman seated on a throne, representing Columbia. Uncle Sam sits behind her, his hand cupped to his ear. The caption reads "Uncle Sam: Whoever you decide for Columbia, you'll be in the wrong."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Peace. A dove offers an olive branch to a bald eagle.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An elderly man sits at a desk, drinking ice water and reading a book, representing a prohibitionist. Another man (St. Paul) stands in front of him, surrounded by clouds and holding a wine glass. The caption reads "St. Paul (to prohibitionist): Drink no more water but take a little wine for your stomach's sake."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Taria, Judas, and General Benedict Arnold are seated, as members of the Betrayer's Club. A devil brings an older man before them. The caption reads "Allow me, Taria, Judas and General Benedict Arnold to present a new member, Senator Littlefool of Wisconsin."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sailors from the USS New York carry baskets of bottles and wine glasses, and one holds a mop. An officer talks with Mrs. Josie Daniels. The caption reads "Aunt Josie Daniels: After you throw overboard all them decanters and wine glasses you can set the crew to polishing the ships hand mirrors and scent bottles. -- Aye aye mam."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A scene in Egypt. 2 men sit at a table, one Egyptian, the other in Roman clothes but with stars on the top hat. The Egyptian is dropping pearls into a drinking cup. The caption reads "Cleopatra dissolving the pearls of tradition - in grape juice."

did.unittitle: "Mrs. Daniels: Yes Miss Columbia, I know all the others are allowed their punch but my Jack needs a mother's care."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An outdoor party. A young woman with stars on her hat (representing Columbia) talks with a older woman (Josie Daniels), who is holding the arm of an angry young man representing the Navy. Columbia is offering a cup to the Navy, and Mrs. Daniels is refusing it for him. The scene also includes several figures drinking from their cups: a young man representing the Army, 2 boys representing the State Interior, and a boy representing the Post Office who is being served the punch by Uncle Sam.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A woman representing Germany, carrying a flag "Kultur," points Attila the Second forward. He rides at the head of an army, with the soldiers depicted as pigs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Busts of 5 figures, the first Uncle Sam and the other four unidentified, each with a bird. A poem about Roman mythology links them to Jupiter, Venus, Minerva, Vergil, and Juno.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An older man turns his back on a sinking ship. A woman, representing the United States, looks over him at the ship. The caption reads "Pacifist: Now don't get excited over the Laconia, Columbia - just remember how calm you was about the Lusitania."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Parody of a Velasquez painting. William Taft dressed as a young girl, with two men behind him dressed as women, there to educate him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man tries to control a runaway horse and cart, which is full of barrels and boxes. The horse, Congress, is running away from France rather than towards it.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A young boy in robes passes between two rows of bowing men.

did.unittitle: "Europe Sacrifices to the Prince of Peace"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A pile of bodies before a cathedral. Military and church leaders officiate.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam dreams of a woman offering drinks from a bowl of Peace to soldiers representing Austria, France, England, Belgium, Russia, and Germany.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Germany, depicted as a stout, plain woman, dances with a gentleman in striped pants, possibly representing the United States. Pretty young women representing England, France, Russia, and Belgium and a young man representing the U.S. Navy watch.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two frames showing the evolution of 2 men. The first evolves from English (1632) to Yengese (1760) to Yankee (1916), retaining his handsome figure and stylish dress. The second evolves from Prussien (1914) to Autr'chien (1915) to Germ-Ameri"chien (1916), becoming fatter, bald, and needing glasses.

did.unittitle: "The Professor: Now boys, that shadow tells me we are approaching a masterpiece....!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A professor leads students through an art museum. He sees a shadow on the wall and thinks it is a classical statue of a woman. They are shocked to come around the corner and find instead a contemporary woman.

did.unittitle: "If they acted the way they feel. Russian dancers and German singers rehearsing together."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Performers on stage for the opera Aida. Pairs of dancers (Russian) and singers German) fight with each other, pulling hair and punching.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Diplomatic dinner where the presiding diplomat Miss Green has put up walls across the table so that Britons, Russians, and Prussians could have dinner together without an incident.

did.unittitle: "The Barg Carmeister and the Lady who was unprepared."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A masked Prussian soldier enters a woman's bedroom with a drawn sword. The woman, representing the United States, is in bed with a gun in each hand.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A Prussian soldier with a flag "Gott mit Uns" marches with his sword drawn. He walks on women lying on the ground with their babies.

did.unittitle: "Kindermorder. Whom the king delighteth to honor."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Young soldiers and sailors climb steps to receive medals from commanding officers. One holds a pair of baby shoes behind his back. Another points to a ship on the horizon.

did.unittitle: "Orderly: Majestat here is still another note from that American President. - Answer it yourself it yourself Fritz. - What shall I say, Majestat. - O, anything."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A soldier brings a letter from the U.S. President to Attila the Second. The commander tells the soldier to reply to the letter himself and say whatever he wishes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Attila the Second stands in front of a throne, ordering his mother away. He is surrounded by soldiers lifting their hats to him and military leaders. The caption reads "He repudiates all who presume to advise him - mother."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Attila the Second runs from a woman, representing France, who holds a rifle with a bayonet and kicks at him. His army, depicted as pigs, run in front of him. The caption reads "Battle of the Marne. He intends to occupy Paris but changes his mind."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sitting before a group of people, Attila the Second paints a portrait of himself with his left hand, plays the organ with his right hand, writes with his left foot, and stirs a cooking pot with his right foot. A military commander reads a book "Me & Krupp" while a church leader reads a book "Me & God." The caption reads "He instructs his ignorant subjects in gunnery, theology, cookery, harmony, short hand, portraiture and the sciences."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An animal trainer addresses a crowd of families. Next to him, 2 men in a cage strain against their chains. The sign on the cage reads "Felis Hohenzollern." The caption reads "Animal trainer: Here we 'ave the Prussian Hyena and cub -- the most blood thirsty brutes in captivity."

did.unittitle: "Has your majesty any message to send to the wounded and dying? You might tell them that their Kaiser never forgets to drink to their health."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A young soldier approaches Attila the Second to get a message for the wounded soldiers. Attila is seated at a table, eating a feast and drinking heavily. He gives the message that he is drinking to the health of the wounded soldiers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The crown prince and a military officer are captured and tied to trees in a Native American village. Their captors scalp a man, put a dog into a cooking pot, smoke a pipe, and drink from one of the Prussian helmets. The caption reads "Captured crown prince: But, papa, this is infamous, it's not civilized warfare!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two politicians (depicted as the walrus and the carpenter) try to get the Democratic voters (depicted as oysters) to vote for them instead. However, the oysters will not be swayed and march with a sign "Here we go in a row - We Want Woodrow."

did.unittitle: 'Whenever he visited England, he would tell his Uncle what a poor thing the British fleet was."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Military commanders and a few ladies view a long line of ships.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Attila the Second speaks before the Pope, who smiles at him. The room is filled with church leaders, most of whom are smiling as well. The caption reads "On a visit to Rome he teaches Pope Leo theology."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Couples, all of them stout, sit at tables and drink from mugs. A military official walks by, looking at them out of the corner of his eye, and a newspaper boy holds a paper reading "Morge Blatter."

did.unittitle: "France (chef d'orchestra): Now, all together. Hindenburg, Tirpitz, William and Son: Allona, enfanta de la Patrie-e!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A woman (representing France) plays a piano and directs four men to sing from a book labeled "La marseillaise, Rule Britannia, Hail Columbia." The men are identified as Hindenburg, Tirpitz, William and Son.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three men, including Attila the Second, sit on a porch with rifles. Women are dragging farm animals in front of them to be shot. The caption reads "He was a mighty sportsman. No beast however fierce escaped him."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Attila the Second, surrounded by smiling military officials, rips up the Treaty with Belgium over a trash can. The caption reads "When inconvenient to keep his word of honor he puts it in the waste-basket with other scraps of paper."

did.unittitle: "What might have happened if H.I.M. occupied a frozen trench for a day or two."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Attila the Second stands in a trench, waist deep in water floating with empty food cans. He remembers the luxury of his camp and waves a flag reading "I freeze! I starve! I surrender!" His soldiers watch him, smiling.

did.unittitle: "The Life of Attila the Second. Chapter 1."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Attila the Second in his cradle, holding onto the skirts of a woman representing Germania. Men representing Insanity, Cruelty, and Vanity offer him gifts. Women representing Honor, Humanity, and Culture turn their backs on him. The caption reads "Ancestral traits crop out in the nursery."

did.unittitle: "The Life of Attila the Second. Chapter 11"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Attila the Second, dressed as a boy, cracks a whip as he rides a wagon (Prussia) pulled by several angry or resigned boys. The boys represent Baden, Wurtemburg, Bavaria, Saxony, and Hanover. Adults representing England, Russia, Spain, and Italy all watch, concerned. The caption reads "Even as a boy he could show his little cousins how to run things."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cardinal Billsey, weeping, is lead away by two angry men. He looks towards another angry man, Aldrich. The caption reads "Cardinal Billsey: O Aldrich, Aldrich, had I but served the State with half the zeal I served my king, it had not left me naked to mine enemies. Henry VIII."

did.unittitle: "Roman History. The suffrage geese of Juno arouse the guardians of the capital."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Geese, representing suffragists, honk loudly and carry signs about getting the vote, waking two sleeping men in front of the Senate, probably President Woodrow Wilson and Secretary of State William Jennings Bryan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A dog with a man's head strains against his leash and growls at a dog across the river.

did.unittitle: "If you must punish murderers, why neglect the biggest ones?"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson pushes a small man in a sombrero while a woman representing civilization tries to direct his attention towards three men with "U-18" on their shirts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three historical figures as the three fates of Roman mythology, spinning a thread of votes instead of life. Attila the Second is Clotho, who spins the thread. Two unidentified men are Lachesis (who measures the thread) and Atropos (who cuts the thread, possibly Theodore Roosevelt). The cut end of the thread reads Nov 4.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Depicts four figures in costumes suited for their work: "The Dancing Faun - evening demi-toilette for a Mayor of New York, The Aspasia - Presidential regalia when coercing the Senate, The Bellona - knockabout for an ex-President, and The Antinous - Coiffure for a Labor Leader."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson, dressed as Marie Antoinette, talks with the leader of the Navy. Outside the window, average citizens are getting angry. The caption reads "The people begin to murmur at Queen Marie Wilsonette and her Minister of Marine."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson and U.S. Ambassador to Berlin James Gerard dressed as girls playing on a see-saw. The Kaiser ("Willie") stands in the middle to keep the two busy and "up in the air."

did.unittitle: "Miss Woodrina: Samuel, your copy gets weaker and weaker. - Oh, I'm tired copyin' these same old words."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson, as a female school teacher, is directing Uncle Sam to write "Peace at any price" over and over on the chalkboard.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A surgeon in Renaissance garb operates on a patient's mouth. The caption reads "The delicate nuances of the French language can find no fitter exemplar than the courtly phrases with which Perré, the court chirurgeon, comforted the Balafré (scarred) who had fallen- 'Now _ you _ _ _ _ swallow This' (Brantôme)."

did.unittitle: "A revival of The Mikado (by Sig. Teddi & Signora Carli)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Theodore Roosevelt and "Carli" (possibly Carl Schurz) as characters from the opera The Mikado. The caption reads "Mikado: My morals were thought by all to be particularly correct... Katisha: But nothing at all, compared to those of his Daughter-in-law-elect! as tough as a bone with a will of her own is his Daughter-in-law-elect"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson in a robe, with Uncle Sam behind him, watches a crowd of men stabbing each other with two volcanoes erupting in the background.

did.unittitle: "Senator Hoare, John Sherman, David Bennett Hill, Zach Chandler and other Senators view the question of the admission of Hawaii to United States in 1983."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several U.S. senators examine, through a large magnifying glass labeled "partisanship," the Hawaiian Question, which is represented as a small rodent on a blank piece of paper. The senators include Steward, Dolph, Chandler, and Hill.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A memorial to former president Benjamin Harrison. A cherub in suit coat and carrying a top hat adds another wreath to a pile around the monument. The wreath has ribbons with the words "patriotism" and "honor."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An elderly gentleman, Levi Morton, sits on a porch. The Presidential Bee buzzes in his ear and he is drinking a "Popularity Spring Tonic."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two men look at the city of Chicago. The caption reads: "Stranger (in Chicago): The streets here seem to be very much alike! Chicagoan: Nonsense, man! The variety is infinite! Now, on that street you'll get sand-bagged; on that street you'll be brass-knuckled; one block down you'll get knock-out drops; and around the corner you'll be garroted. Why, a man can easily tell where he is in Chicago on the darkest nights!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A department clerk in an office in Washington, D.C. works at his desk. He is behind a wall with spikes and the note "Civil Service Reform Law" across the top and a notice "No Political Assessments to be Levied in this Office." He looks up, worried, as a stout gentleman reaches over the wall to hand him a paper reading "Financial Secretary's Appeal" and in the other hand holds a large hat full of money. The caption reads "Have you any suggestions to make which would be beneficial to Republicanism?"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man, dressed like Napoleon, sits on an island. The island represents Ohio and the chair the governorship. He is crumpling the McKinley Bill in his hand. A sun labeled protection is setting in the background.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hill, depicted as a boy, sits on a broken machine labeled "Machine Nomination for Gov. of N.Y." and holds an ax as a turkey labeled "Presidential Nomination 1896" flies away. Hill cries and holds one of the turkey's feathers in his hand, labeled defeat.

did.unittitle: "William Steward, John P. Jones and other "silver" Senators in the guise of an old prospector, stifle discussion on the question of government purchase of silver."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A silver prospector, representing several U.S. senators including Steward and Jones, stands in front of his mine strangling Uncle Sam and bringing him to his knees. Uncle Sam holds a cane reading "Public Opinion." The cartoon refers to senators who where stifling discussion on the issue of if the government should purchase silver.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A graveyard, with each tombstone bearing the date and name of a boom. The most recent is the Hill Boom, 1892, with the epitaph "Since so soon t'was done for, I wonder what t'was begun for." Other booms include Greely (1872), Butler (1884), Hancock (1880), Douglas (1860), McClennan (1864), and Seymour (1868). Tammany Hall, the center of the New York City Democratic political machine, is in the background.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam throws two men out of the Senate, Mitchell and Depew.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam as band leader trying to coax horse representing the Navy down from a telephone pole to join Army, Air Forces, and Strategy in pulling the "U.S. Unified Forces" Band Wagon.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Murphy rests leaning against a large tiger, representing Tammany Hall, lying on a bed made of large contracts. Murphy smokes, and in the smoke is an image of a man named Tweed with a bag of money.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: William Randolph Hearst walks next to an animal labeled "New Party." The animal has a donkey head, elephant body, and a striped cat tail.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A frowning, disproportioned man in a suit. He has a large belly and very small legs. On his lapel are the words "ich daen."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man stands at New York Southhampton harbor with luggage, all marked with the seal H.R.M. and a crown, and with his dog. The dog collar reads "From Prince Wales." A crowd of people run away from him towards a sign that reads "Bacon." Only 2 ragged boys stay, laughing at him.

did.unittitle: "At last his fiendish dream has come true"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A caricature of a Chinese man holds a bloody dagger in one hand and a smoking pipe in the other (possibly referring to opium), standing over several collapsed bodies. The smoke from the pipe makes the word "triumph."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Large men in grass skirts representing numerous trusts march with an American flag and the sign "All we want is 4 years more." The figures represent the iron, steel, flour, canned goods, paper, leather, rubber, bread, and other trusts. They step on little people as they walk. One figure has also stepped through the Constitution.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An older man with glasses and sleeves rolled up stands at a table with a box labeled "Nov 1905" and points at 3 dolls labeled "Gas Comm," "R.R. Comm," and "Rapid Transit Comm." A younger man stands in the background, hat in his hand and a feather duster on the floor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pyotr Stoylpin, a Russian politician drags a woman representing Liberty by the hair from a building labeled "Duma" (the Russian legislative assembly).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A large man, Murphy, sits on City Hall. In his pocket are contracts, gas trust, the Board of Aldermen, and the Police Department. At his feet is a dog named Tammany.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam in a bathing suit walks along a beach. Across the water, in Greece, soldiers in chariots and on foot run on the beach in front of a forest labeled "tall timber."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two men, Taft and Hughes, saw chunks off of a log. They are working on New York. Sections of West Virginia, Nebraska, Indiana, and Ohio have already been cut off.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: At a circus, a tiger holds a woman and a girl under its paws, and an elephant steps on another woman. In the background, a few men hold back a crowd of people.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two men and a tiger fly in a hot air balloon (The Gas Trust), which is shot down by the Gas Investigation. The escaping air forms the word Scandal $. The men are identified as John D. and Murphy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man representing the Star Company of N.J. sits in the Corporation Trust Co. office and writes an editorial for a New York newspaper entitled "Down with Trusts!" The office houses the trusts for ice, leather, meat, hearst and others. A second man looks over his shoulder.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam about to swat at a mosquito representing Castro, which is biting him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A can of "Fresh Canned Meats" sits on top of human bones. Vultures circle the can, and one sits on it.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Numerous consequences of drinking too much whiskey. Men are driven to violence, put in prison, and die and their wives and children are evicted from their homes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The New York fire chief receives a laurel crown with the word honor from a woman representing Fame. A crowd cheers him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An athlete poses in the center. To his right, a stout older man sits on his chair and smokes a pipe. To his left, a decorated military man tries to lift a small weight, sweating.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam is ushered into The Hague.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man and woman try to give a scroll labeled "Russian Liberty" to Uncle Sam, but he turns his back and will not take it.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Joe drilling into a barrel labeled "Standard Oil."

did.unittitle: "Trying to fix up his pet hobby"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man tries to fix a broken rocking horse labeled "Peace" with planks labeled "The Hague."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam stands guard with a gun behind the "Philippine Hen House," ready to attack. A small man in patched clothes and an oversized military helmet approaches with a sack.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam stands guard between a gentleman and three men lead by a man carrying a bucket of mud and wearing a suit made of cards.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam, with torn clothes and tears in his eyes, tries to offer an invitation to the honorable W.R.H., but is ordered to leave.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam reads "Parkers Letter."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The hand of the law points at a skeleton wearing a robe labeled "Carelessness." Behind him, a figure representing the Powder Trust looks at the bodies of several men killed in an explosion.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man in a fancy uniform with lots of metals, and a crown pulled over his eyes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A woman representing Columbia, with a stars and stripes dress, puts a laurel wreath on the head of a man in a military uniform.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam is tied hand and foot by two miniature men. The rope is the Trans Continental Rail Roads and the R.R. Capitol.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: At a circus, Taft and Fairbanks run on a ramp to try to jump over an elephant and land in a bowl labeled "Presidential Nomination."

did.unittitle: "In Union there is strength. You can fool them all some of the time."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man in a striped prison uniform hugs a man in a suit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man in a grass skirt (used in another Davenport cartoon to represent trusts) grabs a man by the throat and takes his money while a police officer looks the other way.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man in a suit, with a whip in his hand, orders a man representing labor to put his vote in the ballot box.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man, Gompers, stands on a stage in front of a crowd of men and instructs them to support Bryan.

did.unittitle: "The Journal thinks his design will in general work in trust circles before the year is out."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man representing "The Journal" leads a man in a striped prison uniform representing the "Ice Trust" from the court house, passed a crowd of happy women and children.

did.unittitle: "Here is where the American people have another chance. If enough of them pull than they can beat the trusts."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A tug of war with Uncle Sam leading the American people on one side and a man in a money suit leading the coal, sugar, and meat trusts on the other side.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A courtroom scene.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A woman representing peace stands in Port Arthur, filled with skulls and cannons, reaching out towards two groups of ships headed towards each other.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A woman representing Civilization is chained by England's loans, jealousy of the powers, apathy, and russia's extension. Bones and a vulture are at her feet.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A worn woman with an empty shopping basket pulls a young girl passed a shop with fresh foul for sale.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: McKinley, holding a declaration of independence, walks with a tall African man who has a bag of money. They walk by "the McKinley," a "Harem maintained by the U.S." One of the harem women holds a paper that reads "vote for McKinley."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An emaciated man on a bed is fed a spoon of "Fever, used by US Army" by a skeleton wrapped in red tape. All the man has to eat is hardtack, and on the floor is a notice that his furlough is refused.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A crying woman carries a baby and leads a young girl, walking along a fence. All the trees are dead, there are a few bones on the ground, and two vultures in the distance.

did.unittitle: "Mr. Hanna as he would like to be caricatured."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A large man, Mr. Hanna, with a halo over his head puts his hand on the head of a poor boy. With the other hand, he pours coins into the basket of a poor old man.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man, tears in his eyes, looks at the Statue of Liberty.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An old man on his knees, praying. At his feet, small figures of soldiers fight and die, and there are scattered letters of sympathy to families of dead soldiers from several countries, including Great Britain, Germany, America, and France.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A tiger smoking a cigar lies on a pile of money in the city treasury.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man in a robe with military metals stands on the edge of the water. Waves roll towards him, with the crests made of men reading towards him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: William Randolph Hearst draws a picture of a lizard with a man's head. On the lizard is written "16 to 1, anti-imperialism, government ownership R.R., anti-injunction."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A poor old man smiles and drinks a cup of coffee. The caption read "It means only a few cents to you. Think what it means to him."

did.unittitle: "The ones that really suffer from the coal strike are never represented in its discussions"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An owner and laborer shout at each other and raise their fits in the air, with a paper reading "We Will Strike" on the table. A woman with five children sit and wait to hear their fate.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Russian General Kuropatkin stands holding a torn flag, surrounded by rifles pointing at him.

did.unittitle: "Spain blew up 266 of my boys for which he got enough to last him but here is Armour who poisened thousands what shall I do?"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam holds a picture of Armour, responsible for the poisoning of thousands of Americans, wondering how to punish him. A picture of a man representing Spain, beaten and bandaged from the punishment he received for killing 266 Americans, is in the background.

did.unittitle: "Joe Chamberlain's War - it comes high but they must have it."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A stout man holds a bill for Joes War in terms of New York Debt ($300,000) and a new war tax ($5,500 per year). He looks at a tall, thin man. In the background, soldiers are shot in battle and a woman mourns in a cemetery crowded with tomb stones.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newspaper publisher William Randolph Hearst walks by with a locked case full of "campaign documents," while William Jennings Bryan observes, shocked.

did.unittitle: "A combination that didn't hit strong up state"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: William Randolph Hearst and a man in a military uniform stand with a tiger on a dirt road. Two farmers look at them suspiciously.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: William Randolph Hearst punches a small man representing the "common people" while two fat men representing Trusts laugh.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Face of a smiling man.

did.unittitle: "Perhaps this is why some of us favor Japan."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man on a horse, sword drawn, with a flag that has a two-headed winged creature. The horse stands on a field of human skulls.

did.unittitle: "Gentlemen, may I ask you as a cash appeal to vote for my friend the Hon [name illegible] Loan?"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A businessman presents a scholar. A fat man in a suit with dollar signs claps in the background.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A giant man, representing religion, holds a child by the foot with one hand and a whip in the other. Small people at his feet run from him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newspaper publisher J. Pulitzer sits with William Jennings Bryan, who is frowning. A paper on the floor reads "The World has sharply disagreed with Mr. Bryan. It has nothing to retract, defend or excuse."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man points two school boys towards Oxford College.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: William Randolph Hearst about to fall off of a bucking Democrat donkey in front of the Democratic Club N.Y. DeLancey Nicoll watches from the club, laughing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man in a suit stands in the center with American flags behind him. To his left sit Uncle Sam and an older man, both smiling. To his right stand two men in business suits smoking cigars, looking angry.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: William Randolph Hearst sits in a chair, looking at a ghostly image of himself.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: William Randolph Hearst as a one man band.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: William Randolph Hearst in silhouette, with the shadow of another man in glasses behind him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: William Howard Taft as the nursery rhyme "Little Jack Horner." He sits in a corner with a "presidential pie," and pulls out a plum, pleased with himself.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam holding a copy of "New York Journal" with headline, "American canal for American people, Senate vote 65 to 17." Refers to decision under Theodore Roosevelt to build Panama Canal.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: William Howard Taft stands to speak at an event, looking at a cheering crowd.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam smiles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A roll of paper representing the "Income Tax Extension Proposal" is warned not to cross the line of March 15 by a Republican elephant in a prison guard uniform with a large gun.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man in a torn suit, Reyburn, carries another man in pilgrim clothes (Penn), tied in rope.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bernard Baruch and three unidentified men, all dressed as musketeers. Baruch hands them a document with a seal representing an atom, possibly referring to his role on the United Nations Atomic Energy Commission.

did.unittitle: "Hot Stuff for the Political Gossip Column"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Adlai Stevenson courts an older woman representing the 1956 Presidential Election, taking her to the movies and a soda fountain.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Adolph Hitler pulls on a lion's tail, representing England, expecting a "lightning war." Another man in a tree shouts "No - No! Adolph -- It's Two Jerks of a Lamb's Tail!"

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink; blue wash, and white tempera corrections, 18 x 18

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Six panels depicting gradual deflation of "Willkie Hopes" (Wendell Willkie, 1940 Republican Presentation candidate).

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink, black crayon, and white tempera, 15 x 17

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Factories in background across lagoon booming "under FDR's Management" and frog labeled "Willkie's Promises" (Wendell Willkie, 1940 Republican Presidential Candidate) on sinking soapbox saying, "But I can do better if -- if -- if -- if...."

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink on pebbleboard, 15 1/2 x 17

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Figure of "Power Trust" holding money bags and backed up by mounds of toys bawls as Uncle Sam gives TVA as present to deprived "Consumer."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man in armor representing "Pacific War" dictates to a woman representing "History," who types on two typewriters at once, one on Java and one on Burma. A stack of completed books sit next to her, including the Invasion of Borneo, Singapore, Guam & Wake, MacArthur, and Pearl Harbor.

did.unittitle: "Boys, Here's A Special Deliver From General MacArthur"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam shows a rush letter he received from General MacArthur to a group of factory workers. MacArthur indicates that the success or failure of modern wars depends primarily on the resources.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A caricature of a Japanese soldier eats part of the globe representing the "good earth of Far East," looking at a time bomb planted in the Philippines by General MacArthur.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink and black crayon on pebbleboard, 13 x 16

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Worker wearing cap of "Defense Program" and flexing bulging bicep of "Detroit's Mass Production Facilities."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A soldier rides a tank labeled "Nazi War Machine" with a "Just Married" sign and a map of Italy on the back.

did.unittitle: "Have you mark'd but the fall o' the Snow before the Soil hath smutched it?" -- Ben Johnson,

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink, black crayon on newsprint, 14 1/2 x 19

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Stooping man waiting at bus stop in a snowstorm. Duffy was of some renown, winning three Pulitzer Prizes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A panting figure representing the thirsty Reich War Machine reaches out towards the Rumanian oil fields.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A robed figure representing history writes in a book about battles in 1940 and at the end of the page writes "finished." In its other hand, the figure holds a copy of the British-French alliance.

did.unittitle: "Sand for the Biggest of Incendary Fires"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam stands on the edge of a cliff pouring a bag of sand representing the $70 billion war budget for 1942-43 to try to put out the fire of war.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man representing India sits peacefully next to a burning paper labeled "Cripps Plan," while across the Bay of Bengal an enemy soldier approaches with a bloody sword.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three football players labeled "Dayton, Cincinnati, and Ohio-U" bumping heads.

did.unittitle: "Mr. Woeful Goes to the President's Ball,"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Nine panels showing reluctant "Mr. Woeful" being coaxed into going to the ball.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink, black crayon on pebbleboard, 9 x 13

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flashlight of "Public Interest" shining into a padlocked ballot box for "School Bond Election."

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink, black crayon, and white tempera corrections, 12 x 14

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wendell Willkie (1940 Republican Presidential candidate) throwing boomerang shaped "Attacks on FDR" that bounce back to hit GOP.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam, his head shaped like the United States, sits in a dentist chair. Leon Henderson, as the dentist, removed the Sugar Supply tooth as part of rationing.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink and black crayon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: GOP elephant building platform "Reserved for the Nation's Burdens After January 20." out of "faulty" Republican materials.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink and black crayon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: GOP elephant using rubber stamp "used regularly every four years" which reads "Help the Farmer."

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with white tempera, 12 x 14

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: John L. Lewis for the C.I.O. in "Willkie For President" washtub (Wendell Willkie, 1940 Republican Presidential candidate) exhorting Uncle Sam to jump from "stability of the New Deal" to join him on "troubled waters."

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink and black crayon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: F.D.R. nailing up "GOP Falsifications and Hysteria" he has shot down with the "Facts!"

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink, black crayon, and white tempera, 12 x 14

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Jack-o-lantern with Wendell Willkie's (1940 Republican Presidential candidate) initials attempting to scare the public.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink and black crayon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: GOP elephant slinging mud at Democratic donkey who pays no attention.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A miniature man, representing Congressmen who voted self-pensions, reads a newspaper with the headline "Sponsors of Bundles for Congress Decide to Call Off Caravan." He is relieved, thinking it would have made him look small if that had passed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Winston Churchill sits on a locked safe labeled the "British Cabinet." A lion, representing the movement to unseat him, circles the cabinet and growls.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam, reading a newspaper with headlines about the Rubber Question, Sugar Question, Weather, and Business as Usual, is surprised by a spike labeled "Surrender of Singapore."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam is being fed medicine of "Bad News" by the hand of War. War adds some sugar of MacArthur's Continued Stand to sweeten up the medicine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Abraham Lincoln watches the earth burn.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An armored President Taft exhorts Root, Penrose, Gallinger, Bannes, and Camron to "buckle on [their] armor for the battle of humanity and the common people that must be fought," while the armor of Vermont and Utah lies on the floor.

did.unittitle: "Europe: as the personally conducted Domines described it,"

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with blue pencil

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Graphic shows city of cathedrals around central square. Cameo shows a large man describing the scene to two older ladies.

did.unittitle: "Democratic Sanitarium. Morphine and other drug habits cured."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In a sanitarium for democrats with drug habits, President Woodrow Wilson stands at the center of a group of men holding a bottle of "Tariff Subsidy Morphine," which all the others reach for. On the time are also high protection cocaine and dope.

did.unittitle: "Wall Street, 'The Index of the Country's Prosperity.'",

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with blue pencil

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Graphics show "Flim Flam Finance" manipulating the thermometer of the economy with fire and ice alternately.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Beautiful young lady confronts a dyspeptic young man.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson in a gym, trying to get an overweight man representing Protection Trust back into shape. One of the exercise machines is "Tariff Reform Chese Weights."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Saint Peter directs a reluctant man toward Heaven where an angel applies wings and distributes harps.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with blue pencil

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sinister China consoles desperate Morocco sitting on the steps of Algiers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Boy prince reviews a bevy of prospective brides being led in by a military figure as a garish ecclesiastical figure and the stern-faced mother look on. Appears to be set in Italy or Spain.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A pleased Uncle Sam monitors the embarkation of American troops from the Philippines at the end of the Spanish-American War and ushers in the "civilizing" forces of American womanhood who confront the awestruck natives.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Men conversing at a bar.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with blue pencil

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A conductor gives a harsh look to a horn player during a performance.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Figure in monk's habit holding cross appears before bar of "German Justice" which is presided over by Hitler sitting in chair of "Religious Persecution."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: FDR backed up by obese figure of "That 20 Billion Dollar Congress" calling equally fat "Corporations" -- "Economic Royalist."

scopecontent.p: A large hand representing Nazi Germany bushes a soldier representing Japan forward.

origination.label: Creator
origination.persname: Enright, Walter Joseph (American cartoonist and illustrator, born 1879)

odd.p: Made for Democratic National Committee, 1940 Campaign.

origination.label: Creator
origination.persname: Enright, Walter Joseph (American cartoonist and illustrator, born 1879)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A large figure representing the Petain Government sits with his back facing the harbor, holding the French fleet there with strings. A large figure representing the Nazi's is emerging from the water to attack.

origination.label: Creator
origination.persname: Enright, Walter Joseph (American cartoonist and illustrator, born 1879)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A large soldier representing Japan kneels on a map of Japan with one hand reaching for China and another reaching towards Pacific islands.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam floats in a rough sea labeled "Foreign Troubles" with a "Wilson" life preserver. An elephant swings at him with a club labeled "War."

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with white tempura on textured paper. 22 1/4 x 29 1/2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Huge "Pork Barrel" (pig in a barrel) eats money from a "national resources" trough as determined Uncle Sam looks on holding knife marked "Army-Navy plans."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man puts up a brick wall representing the McCarran Exclusion Act in front of the Statue of Liberty.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Joseph McCarthy throws smears, lies, and fake charges from the safety of the U.S. Senate.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several figures sit around a camp fire in front of a tent representing United Europe. A man representing Great Britain sits alone at his own fire.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Laval, represented as a vulture, sits on a cactus shaped like a Nazi swastika.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man representing the 8,700,000 unemployed is buried underneath a city.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A mountain made of people. A wall representing the Minimum Wage and Hour Law keeps people from sliding too far down the mountain.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink and black crayon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Caricature of large man leaning on pillar in pose typical of late nineteenth century Robber Barons.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man in military uniform (General Leonard Wood). Tags indicate that his army, sword, and uniform are gifts from Theodore Roosevelt.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A large man crushes a working man under his thumb with one hand and pours out a chest of old clothes for him with the other.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ghoulish figure of "MOB" threatening Atlanta who attempts to protect herself with shield of "Broken Justice".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three half drunken Dutchmen carousing in farmyard.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink and black crayon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam looks sternly on as a caricature of FDR's "intellectual" aide broadcasts "Provocation, Defiance, and War Talk" to the Nation.

scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam enthroned in chair, with "Andy" (Carnegie) dressed as a fool, at his feet. "The court jester-sometimes his prattle becomes a trifle wearing."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson addresses three figures representing the Trust Question, Currency Question, and Mexican Question, standing in the middle of the aftermath of a party.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Andrew Carnegie as a cherub or gargoyle looking out at the setting sun. Two birds overhead.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: J. Bull seated on doorsteps looking across the street at Uncle Sam sweeping. Cartoon refers to Boer war exposures: fake fire arms, bad ammunition, supply contracts, army scandals, and embalmed beef.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: William Howard Taft with a box of "Ohio bonbons" in his lap with a GOP elephant seated in a hammock.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Andrew Carnegie rides barrel of Tariff Reform wagon. Figures representing trusts for steel, wool, coal, lumber, paper, and beef jubilate with "protection booze."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man representing South America walks with a small woman. Her apron is labeled "Monroe Doctrine" and she carries a bottle labeled "Wilson." Uncle Sam stands behind them, looking thoughtful.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Amateur night-$10.00 for the best ten minute sermon." Crowds of blackcoated clergymen mill around pulpit entrance.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Former president Taft hands new president Woodrow Wilson a baby shooting a guy who represents the Mexican Situation.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink, blue pencil used in the background, 10 x 12 1/2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Man seated on a chair with child-sized woman on his lap holding candies labeled "To Ida from Jack". A bouquet for Ida sits on the table.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Professional politician stands between two composite animals combining parts of the elephant (GOP) and donkey (Democrat) to form one animal representing Reaction Republicanism and Democracy and the other representing Progressive Republicanism and Democracy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Free-for-all in congress with fighters labeled De Armond, Sulzer, Fitzgerald, Gardiner, Goldfogle.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Man leaning over desk with large club labelled "Republican Machine" and "House Insurgents" with wings and halos in the background.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bottle shown with head of Uncle Joe (brand) label, the other with Aldrich.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bill Bryan seated in a chair with a bottle (Perpetual booze candidacy) "Nomination perfecto" in air behind him. Scroll: "I hereby pledge myself to quit liquor and tobacco in any form."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Seven female harridans, of which two are seated in chairs. Pictures of Samson and Delilah and Adam and Eve are hanging on the wall behind them.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam, holding scroll reading "Protest against Russian exclusion of Jewish Americans" opens closet and skeleton wearing sign "American exclusion of Chinese" tumbles out.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Man in bowler hat and cigar depicted as a turkey, stares out over a parapet. His legs are made of celery and below him is a building for selling produce.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A circus. The "Trust Band," lead by Archbold, plays music about owning Roosevelt and the Republican Party. The GOP elephant dances with Taft as the ring master. Theodore Roosevelt is depicted as a monkey in the "Progressive Cage," looked at by Loeb, Penrose, and Hearst.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Jack and the Beanstalk. The giant, representing Monopoly, falls to the ground as Jack (President Woodrow Wilson) cuts the beanstalk (excessive protection) with an ax (tariff revision).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Monopoly" seated at a table eating "benefits of protection" while a man (Underwood) sings him a song about tariff reform.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam as judge in a courtroom. President Woodrow Wilson is brought before the court to be accused by a figure representing a Lobbyist, pointing to a poor family holding "The Market Basket."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A smiling President Woodrow Wilson brushes himself off after a wedding party and addresses three tired figures representing the trust question, currency question, and Mexican question.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson as a traffic cop. He halts cars and wagons representing the Republican machine, peanut politics, Wall Street, opposition press, and Bull Moose so that two elderly ladies representing the Tariff Bill and Currency Bill can cross the street.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam enthroned in chair, with "Andy" (Carnegie) dressed as a fool, at his feet.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Workers stand outside factory reading notice ..."if the Tariff bill is passed, your wages will be cut." Oversized man labeled Redfield holds notice to manufacturers that the Department of Commerce will investigate wage reductions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The GOP Republican elephant walks away from Woodrow Wilson's White House, bag in hand, looking back sadly.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson as a lion tamer. The lion is his secretary of state William Jennings Bryan, and his water dish is full of harmony.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: William Jennings Bryan, depicted as a bird, stands on a perch made from buildings representing Chautauqua and the Commoner, ripping open boxes of "cabinet portfolios" and "ambassador's snaps."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A mother, representing Monopoly, demands a fireman (the New Jersey Legislature) save her child (New Jersey corporation law) from a burning building. The fire represents Governor Woodrow Wilson's Anti-trust crusade.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A young lady huntress with the turkey she has shot.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink and black crayon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: FDR as woman trying to decide whether to buy Easter Bonnet of "Third Term."

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink and black crayon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: FDR holding donkey, shaking money out of its ear saying, "You can't make a silk purse out of a sow's ear - But you can out of a donkey's!"

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink and black crayon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Joe Public" overwhelmed by eight ball of "Roosevelt's Eighth Year."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Street scene outside subway stairs. A rich man is giving money to a blind man in foreground while old lady is knocked aside in the stairwell. Stylish couple passes by in background.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A series of men's faces arranged in vertical stripes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Caricature of industrial capitalist sits on money bag with factories in background labeled "Industrial Depression." The "Wage Earner" prostrates himself before the capitalist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: FDR as King canceling the U.S. Constitution.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Boss" Edward J. Flynn (Democratic National Chairman) concocting recipe for "New Deal Campaign" 1940.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam pointing to portraits of Jackson, Jefferson, and Washington hanging over FDR's desk saying "I've has some darn good men but they refused a third term." FDR insists he's needed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: FDR hiding his book of "3rd Term Politics" behind book of "National Emergencies and U.S. Defense Problems."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: FDR and Uncle Sam on tour of Defense plants but FDR cannot keep his eyes off "The Voting Public."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: FDR wearing his "3rd Term" tuxedo, cuts in on Uncle Sam and Liberty who have been dancing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: FDR addressing the nation while he hides stick of "Dictatorship" behind his back.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Democratic donkey atop 1940 Democratic "Platform" which is shoddily constructed on shaky foundation of "The Seven Year New Deal Record."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: FDR's "3rd Term Band Wagon" pulled up at John L. Lewis' (leader of C.I.O.) steps ready for him. Lewis decided to put his support behind FDR's challenger, Wendell Willkie in the 1940 campaign.

did.unittitle: "Little Bo Peep Has Lost Her Sheep.",

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: FDR as Little Bo Peep searching for sheep (Fellow Democrats) who have fled through hole in party fence made by falling tree of "Rooseveltism."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Armoured FDR riding war steed shouting over radio microphone, "I Hate War!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man uses his cane to poke a fat man who is shouting "Deutschland uber allies! Down mit Wilson!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Thomas Watson, Georgian Democratic politican and writer, being thrown out of Georgia along with his slander sheets by figures of "honest citizen," classical figure of Georgia, the Church, and Uncle Sam. Refers to Watson's publishing of the ROMAN CATHOLIC HIERARCHY in 1910 which caused an uproar.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with brown ink washes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rumpled, stout, and drunk gentleman rolls up to "Paying Teller" who holds slip in his fist and looks sternly at the man.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with white tempera corrections, 22 3/4 x 18 1/2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Prison cell scene with Tom Watson, Georgian Democratic politician and writer, arranging to have figure named Creen kill sleeping figure named Frank.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with white tempera corrections, 20 1/2 x 27 1/2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A small figure, Frank, is pulled into a cave by a large hand representing legal technicalities. Over the cave is written "All Hope Abandon Ye Who Enter Here." A man named Thaw and his millions wave to Frank.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A figure representing the Tin Plate Industry knocks on the door of the "Great American High Tariff Workhouse: 47 1/2 cents charity bestowed on every dollars worth produced." It refers to the McKinley Tariff of 1890, which created an American tinplate industry by high duty on foreign imports.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Adolph Hitler in a tank representing "Totalitarian Conquest" drives up to a detour caused by the Monroe Doctrine, sending him away from the Americas.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Four frame cartoon about rationing. Tires and sugar are scarce, but there are plenty of men registering for military service and defense bonds and stamps to purchase. The cartoon ends with "And remember - the faster we buy bonds and stamps the sooner we will again have all the other good things."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A magic show. A man representing Congress has sawed a woman in half, and is leaving without putting her back together. One half is "neutrality" and the other is "legislation."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A large soldier representing the Nazi's stands on a map of Europe, facing England but looking back at the Mediterranean. Planes fly towards England and northern Africa is full of tanks.

did.unittitle: "They Asked for it and They are Going to Get It! - Knocking Them Right Out of the Picture"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Caricatures of Hitler and two other men are hit by a large fist representing the U.S.A. They see stars labeled troops, planes, tanks, ships, and guns.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: England, Italy, Russia, Germany, and Uncle Sam are waiting to see what FDR will pull out of his "Democratic Convention (1940)" hat.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam absorbed in "Presidential Campaign Crossword Puzzle" sitting atop Hitler's smoldering "Blitzkrieg Volcano."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 Panels-- 1) June 1940, FDR's Campaign Headquarter (New Deal Novelties) alone on street.
2) August 1940, Wendell Willkie (Republican Presidential candidate) has built up his Headquarters and has plans for additions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wendell Willkie (1940 Republican Presidential Candidate) challenging FDR to a debate as he constructs a platform atop his headquarters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The Public" painting Willkie" (Wendell Willkie, 1940 Republican Presidential candidate) across campaign headquarters as "Ole Time Political Boss" looks on.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Delegate to 1940 Democratic National Convention in Chicago being rolled flat by FDR in his "Steamroller."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: FDR riding high above the Democratic donkey with its "Third Term Saddle" on the way to the "Chicago Track" (site of the 1940 Democratic National Convention.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two snakes emerge from a trunk of clothes and look at each other. A woman reads in the background.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man carrying cases in the shape of a banjo and a rifle talks to another man who is seated on a bench.

did.unittitle: "Stephen B. Elkins Entering the Senate to Claim the Seat he has Bought by Liberal Contributions to Party Funds."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Stephen B. Elkins walks through the U.S. Senate with a certificate of election in one hand and a bag of money in the other. He walks by men with signs reading "Smith," "Jones Bonanza Senator," "Teller Senator on Cash Basis," and "Brice Senator by Grace of Boodle."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ladies and gentlemen in fancy clothes stand talking to each other, facing a door. Behind them, a blank sign hides two mice.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man and a woman on a ship's deck run into each other, both dropping a book, as the deck tilts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Theodore Roosevelt depicted as an older woman tries to sweep as a big gust of wind comes in through the window, knocking a picture sideways and blowing open a book.

did.unittitle: 'House Cleaning No. I President Theodore Roosevelt"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Theodore Roosevelt, depicted as a woman in dress and apron, sweeps hard enough to cause the dust to billow.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Taft, depicted as a maid with black dress and white apron, vacuums.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Depiction of a Princeton University football player in 1882 and 1912. The 1882 player is thin, smiles, and has no padding. The 1912 player is muscular, frowns, and has lots of padding.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A woman in a gown begins to climb a staircase in a dark room. A man with a candle looks down from higher up the staircase. A note on the cartoon: copy of picture by Charles Dana Gibson.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Princeton football players are helped off the field by team mates or on stretchers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A young man and woman slide off a log they were sitting on as one end of the log rises up. A dog watches them.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Princeton football. Almost all members of both teams are piled up trying to find the ball, which comes loose. A Princeton player tries to catch it.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Princeton football. Four players on the opposing team hold onto a Princeton player, who has the ball, but they are unable to stop him from moving forward.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A young man in a suit and bowtie.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A woman in a fancy dress comes through curtains.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A young couple dance together at the Officers' Mess, Witchitaw Falls, Texas.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sketches of several men and women. A note on the back indicates that they were drawn when the artist was 13 years old.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Head and shoulders of a young woman. There is a sketch of a young man on the back.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Head and shoulders of a young woman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This imgage appears to be two different cartoons. On the left: two men in suits with one man in mid-fall reaching for his hat. On the right: four figures in hats walking and standing around.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man is hit from behind by a train or trolley car.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two men walk through the woods. One struggles with the weight of several bags and is tripping over a log. The other is carrying a canoe over his head, and the canoe is getting stuck in a tree.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Men and women talk together or sit eating at a party.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man carrying paintbrushes and a palette answers the door to a man with his hat in his hands.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A young woman in fancy clothes stands at a table holding several books, looking away from them.

did.unittitle: "Racing Term - A British on the Speedway"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man leans down to brush the dirt off of his own shadow.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
