scopecontent.p: Two men, one in a three piece suit with striped pants, the other with a coat that has fur on the cuffs and collar, a top hat, and cane.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man climbs onto the thin branch of a tree to try to reach a bird's nest, but his weight causes the branch to bend and an egg to fall from the nest. He also drops a book. The man has a hole in his shoe, and so possible represents Adlai Stevenson.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A sporting event. A stout man is ordered to leave a bench by the usher to make room for a young couple carrying a Princeton flag. A young couple with a Yale flag sit next to him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sketch of a young woman's face.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sketch of a seated young woman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A well-dressed customer in a hardware store asks the clerk behind the desk if he has any small vices. The clerk angrily replies "That's none of your business!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A runner in a striped shirt (possibly a Princeton University athlete) struggles up a hill, with two other struggling runners in the background.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Calvin Coolidge dancing with huge woman labeled "Heavy Presidential Responsibilities" and saying, "If you don't mind, I think I'll sit out the next one!" while prospective candidates gape on from right.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Men in foreground reading frantically of an airplane crash while autos collide in background. Two of the accident prone drivers are conversing. One says, "Read about the airplane crash this morning?" The other answers, "Yes-they're not safe! Too many nuts driving 'em!"

did.unittitle: "He hasn't changed his attitude in 430 years, and he's held that pose for 430 years",

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fat, bearded symbol of the establishment seated at desk saying, "It can't be done!" with back turned to General Mitchell who holds a paper labeled "Future of Air Power." Figures of Columbus, Fulton, Morse, Cyrus, and Langley in background.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hoover as farmer bringing calf labeled "Farm Problem" from dummy cow on left labeled "Political Panaceas & Promises" to cow on right labeled "Economic Realities."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man, Knick, stands in the snow hitting animals wearing top hats that are emerging from a hole in a hill. The hill was the "Public Service Commission" but the public has been crossed out and replaced with "Private."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Caricatures of General Reed Smoot, Field Marshal Penrose, and Major "Pop" Gallinger.

did.unittitle: "Smith and Hoover? by James C. Young"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A large man glowers down at a small GOP Republican elephant, who says "Yes, boss."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: William Jennings Bryan kneels in front of a safe labeled "The Presidency," holding a paper that reads "16 to 1 Populism, anti-imperialism, predatory wealth, and anti-everything." He holds a hand to his ear to ignore four men trying to get his attention about tariff reform: Underwood, Clark, Wilson, and Harmon."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three men (Gorman, Brice, and Smith, Jr.) sit in a cage hung out in front of the Democratic National Headquarters. A sign on the cage reads "Exposed here as a warning for all time - these traitors to democratic principles and satraps of trust and monopoly."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Figure labeled "Miss Democracy" seated in bed looks aghast at a vast array of Christmas presents all bearing visage of William Jennings Bryan.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Black and white version on reverse.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Figure labeled "Miss Democracy" seated in bed looks aghast at a vast array of Christmas presents all bearing the visage of William Jennings Bryan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A crowd of people run from a thin old man in rags labeled T.C.P., carrying a crutch representing the "State Republican Machine" and a bottle labeled "Express Co."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Policeman labeled "Graft Tariff" halts a working class family about to cross the street to a woods labeled "Prosperity" where five fat men labeled "Beef Trust," "Coal Trust," "Steel Trust," "Clothing Trust," and "Lumber Trust" are reveling.

did.unittitle: "'Will you walk into my parlor?' said the spider to the fly.",

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Spider is labeled "interstate Commerce Commission" and the web is labeled "The Law". Fly is Averell Harriman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Aladdin-like figure wearing hat of "Vested Interests" lounges on ottoman as genie figure labeled "the Reputable Press" presents news stories edited to his master's liking.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Aladdin-like figure wearing hat of "Vested Interests" lounges on an ottoman as the genie of "The Reputable Press" presents news stories edited to his master's liking.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Lithograph--black and white on one side, hand colored on the reverse with blue "X's" and "Kill" added from original,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Triumphal procession in which figures labeled Lodge, Foraker, Aldrich, and Shaw bear a container labeled "Dingley Tariff" and are led by figure with upraised arms identified as Cannon. Pious figures to the side are labeled "Trust, Infant Industries, and Protected Monopoly."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An older couple huddles in their bed, holding an umbrella to keep out the water from their leaking roof.

did.unittitle: "They Know the Kind of Decoys to Use",

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Figures of hunters labeled "Public Service Corp." and "Political Boss" crouching in the foreground as decoys labeled "Respectable Candidates attract flocks labeled "Votes."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scene of joyous celebration including speeches, flag waving, and fireworks all about to be crushed by the gigantic thumb labeled "Special Privileges."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scene of joyous celebration including speeches, flag waving, and fireworks all about to be crushed by the gigantic thumb labeled "Special Privileges." Black and white on reverse.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: William Jennings Bryan as the modern Narcissus gazing into a pool labeled "Democratic Sentiment."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Nero-like reclining figure labeled "protected Monopoly" looks out over valley of factories while slain figures symbolizing labor lie at his feet. They hold slips of paper titled, "No Orders Till Prices Come Down, Cancelled Orders, and Consumer Balks." Signed: "Kep with apologies."

did.unittitle: "The Return of the Dove to the Ark"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The ark, labeled "Democratic Principles," is stuck on a mountain as the flood waters of "Republican Power" recede. The Democrat donkey flies to meet the ark, carrying a branch with represents Democratic victories, revolt against the tariff, and reduced Republican majorities. Many politicians on the ark reach out to the donkey, including Wilson, Shepard, Johnson, Harmon, Parker, Watterson, Haven, Core, Gaynor, Folk, Gray, Owen, Tillman, Clark, Mack, and Williams.

did.unittitle: "To the American Soldier the Middle Road is Closed.",

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gigantic Figure of prohibitionist blocking road labeled "Temperance" whereon lies a closed canteen. To either side are roads labeled "Teetotalism" and "Excess."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Figure labeled "Murphy" forces classically garbed lady labeled "N.Y. State Democracy" out onto the street.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Figures labeled "Labor" and "Railroads" bound together by cord of "Public Opinion" and trundled in cart labeled "arbitration" across tightrope over gorge. Cart is pushed by American Patriotic Figure whose tails are held by figure labeled "Seth Law."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam leaning on magnet labeled "United States Protectorates" which he directs towards Central and South American nations symbolized by peasants. Cuba and Panama are already attached to the magnet while Nicaragua and Costa Rica are in midair. Colombia, Salvador, Guatemala, and Honduras cling desperately to palm trees while Mexico explodes in background.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A military surrender. Forces representing "Special Privilege" surrender to President Woodrow Wilson, Secretary of State William Jennings Bryan, and Representative Oscar W. Underwood. The Special Privilege lower flags labeled Railroad Rule, Lobbyism, Banking Trusts, Tariff Monopoly, and Food Adulteration.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson is depicted as a doctor delivering the Tariff Reform and Currency Reform legislation "babies" at the 63rd Congress. Representative Underwood brings cribs for the babies and Uncle Sam waits outside the door as the expectant father.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Benjamin Harrison talking through a beaver skin hat and blowing the top out of it.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson walks up a mountain representing "Administration Problems," carrying his Vice-President Thomas R. Marshall on his shoulders. Marshall waves a gavel and shouts through a megaphone labeled "four years' silence."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Terrified man labeled "Consumer" is bound by cords labeled "Graft Tariff" to a rack at the bottom of a pit while a pendulum labeled "Cost of Living" swings closer and closer.

did.unittitle: "The Charmer: Orpheus Bryan and the Democratic Beasts",

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: William Jennings Bryan as Orpheus strumming a lyre labeled "Harmony" attracting beasts labeled as contemporary political figures.

odd.head: General
odd.p: "Puck," August 5, 1908, volume LXIV, number 1640. Black and white version on reverse. Bryan was the Democratic Presidential Nominee versus Taft.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A woman with a hat labeled "The West" rides a horse at a gallop and swings a bag labeled "Wilson."

did.unittitle: "Speaker Reed, 'King' of the House, Receiving some Opposition from Representative Crisp on his Unprecedented Quorum Rulings."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Representative Crisp as a judge hits Speaker Reed on the heat with a gavel labeled "Firmness," denting Reed's crown.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Democratic leaders Wilson, Underwood, Harmon, Bryan, and Clark as wise men and shepherds in the nativity story, following a star representing the "Tariff Issue."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Skeleton figure with crown and scythe jumping off train labeled "Steel Car".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contestants sling mud at each other upon a stage labeled "Columbus Mayorality Campaign" while an unamused voter watches from the foreground.

odd.head: General
odd.p: "Out in the Cold World" on reverse.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Images of sports and activities at a water resort.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two figures labeled "Isolationist" and "Aid to Britain" fighting in basket atop GOP elephant.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two men fight in an area labeled "Section 10 Press," with a poster for Woodrow Wilson's presidential campaign in the background.

did.unittitle: "It's Going to be a Long Chase"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An old man representing the Democratic Legislature runs after a rabbit representing a balanced budget, sprinkling it with salt (representing the proposed sales tax).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Gomez of Cuba is in a house on fire with rebellion. President Taft is depicted as a fire chief with a hose (intervention), and a member of his crew, representing Wall Street, is offering to turn on the water. The caption reads: "President Gomez - Please, Mr. Fireman, don't turn it on - I'm gettin' it out all right."

did.unittitle: "News Item - The President Says He Will Not Attend the Convention"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In the first frame, President Franklin Delano Roosevelt paces in a circle surrounded by phones and radios, question marks above his head. In the second frame, labeled "The Spirit of Roosevelt," he runs towards Chicago with a bag in each hand and a graduation hat on his head.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt as a fisherman, catching a large fish representing the American People. His bait is labeled "Artificial Bait Designed by the New Deal and Company - A Sucker is Born Every Minute." The lures he is using include Political Relief, Political Social Security, Political Farm Regulations, Political Propaganda, Political Wage-Hour Bills, and Political Strangulation of Business.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several politicians, represented as angels, promise to keep the campaign clean while carrying brushes and bottles to smear each other. The politicians include Barkley, Michelson, Pepper, Minton, and Ickes.

did.unittitle: "A Proud Parent Does a Bit of Broadcasting"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A figure representing Indiana broadcasts praise of Wendell Lewis Willkie, his son, over the radio to the rest of the country, which agrees to support the "youngster" in the presidential race.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A figure representing "truth, wisdom, intelligence, calmness, candor, experience, courage, conscience and sincere love of country" tells Col. Charles A. Lindbergh that he deserves the respect of his country for his "sober opposition to war-hysteria and to meddling in foreign quarrels; patriotic advocacy of a thoroughly strong national defense for America."

did.unittitle: "But the Coattails Don't Hide the Patches"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt looking at the star of a third term. His coattails of "War Mongering" and "War Hysteria" fly up to reveal patches in his pants of the "New Deal's" record.

did.unittitle: "Hooray! We Could Kill Two Birds with One Stone!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Franklin Delano Roosevelt and Paul V. McNutt, depicted as birds on the tree of the Democratic Party, candidates for President and Vice President in 1940. A man representing the American People takes aim with his slingshot (the election), using the 1940 vote as his ammunition, to take out both of them.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt uses taxpayer's money to light a cigar (representing proposed new spending sprees), producing a cloud of smoke representing the "most tremendous national debt in history." A figure representing American Taxpayers voices his discontent. He is wearing a barrel because he is so poor, surrounded by the cigar ends from other spending sprees. Roosevelt ignores a paper with his resolution not to smoke in his economic promises every year from 1933 through 1939.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man representing Indiana looks at several billboards supporting Wendell L. Willkie as the Republican candidate for President in 1940.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt tells "Auntie Democracy" that he doesn't want a third term, represented by an apple, but is licking his lips and the cores from 1932 and 1936 are on the ground.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A ventriloquist's dummy sits amid drums and signs supporting Roosevelt for another four years. The caption reads "And it will start as soon as the big voice of "Edgar Bergen" Roosevelt arrives."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hungry children with empty plates sit at a table "reserved for deserving citizens now on relief" while a man representing "New Deal Government at Washington" feeds the food to dogs under the table representing waste, extravagance, and vote purchasing. A cook representing the American People holds a heavy plate of food representing the increasing tax burdens and says "I wouldn't kick about the serving, if all the food were only consumed by the family it's intended for!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Memorial to Dr. Glenn Frank, with the gratitude of the nation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt as a baseball player, sitting safe on third base.

did.unittitle: "Hitting the Line Hard and Gaining Yardage"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The 1940 presidential race as a football game. President Franklin Delano Roosevelt is tackled by "The Big Seven-Year Line of New Deal Blundering" while Wendel Willkie runs with the ball ("American First" Principles) towards the "goal of Republican success in November."

did.unittitle: "The Careless Fellow who Started a Prairie Fire"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In the first frame, President Franklin Delano Roosevelt calmly rides a donkey representing the "New Deal Party" along the "Long, Long Seven-Year Trail of New Deal Blunders," carelessly throwing a lit cigarette butt representing his third term aspirations into the "dry grass of America's sacred ideals and traditions." In the second frame, the prairie is on fire with the nation's no third term sentiment, and the donkey runs in fear, dragging Roosevelt behind him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The New Deal Government at Washington" is depicted as Ferdinand the Bull, happy sitting under a tree representing "huge spending sprees and waste" and looking forward to the 1940 election.

did.unittitle: "Another One Soon to End His Consecutive Game Record"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt depicted as a baseball player before the 1940 presidential election. He keeps swinging at and missing the ball, representing the depression, because his bat (The New Deal) has a big hole in it from wasteful spending, meddling in business, and "crazy policies." The Republican elephant watches, laughing, while the Democrat donkey watches in tears.

did.unittitle: "But the Spirit Plane Still Flies On"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Memorial for Amelia Earhart. Her name is written in the history book of aviation as her plane flies through the clouds.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A girl shows her doll to a woman, while a boy plays with a toy train on the floor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A wealthy girl turns up her nose at a poor girl, who is carrying a baby on her back.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A young girl kneels to pray, while behind her a woman guides a younger girl towards an empty chair.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A woman sings before an audience. All that is visible of the audience is the back of their fancy hats.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Characters from novels, including Alice in Wonderland and the lion and scarecrow from the Wizard of Oz, run for an elevator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A fireplace at the White House, the logs filled with questions that President Franklin Delano Roosevelt has not answered during his "fireside chats," radio addresses to the nation. The questions are: Why a third term? What is "Aid short of war"? How about unemployment? How do we pay for everything? Where is your foreign policy taking us?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam accuses an idle factory machine, representing "partly idle industry," of slacking.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man and woman read a sign that there will be "no more rubber for girdles or suspenders." She has a thicker waist because she has no girdle and he has his pants tied up with rope.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Treasury, represented as a chicken coop, is robbed by the man who is supposed to be protecting it. He gets $10,000 per year for each Congressman in a "pension grab."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam is stabbed in the back by traitorous acts of Nazi paid Americans.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A woman, representing "the hopefuls," cuddles up to a man representing the presidential nomination for 1940.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The "Good Ark G.O.P." has been built by the Republicans for the predicted flood of anti-New Deal votes. Three pairs of animals come running to get on the ark: Roosevelt Republicans, professional political job hunters, and bolting Democrats.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Congress, depicted as a woman washing dishes representing the Logan-Walter Bill and the Wagner Law Revision, is tempted away from her work by a man with flowers, representing the Fall Campaign.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An "off-the-reservation Congressman" is hit by the administration with a large bag representing the "use of WPA Billions in elections."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The GOP elephant is confused as it is ordered to travel in two different directions by men representing conservative Republicans and liberal Republicans.

did.unittitle: "Got to Grease 'er Up Good for Election Year"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt, depicted as a car mechanic, works on a car representing the New Deal machine. He pumps it full of oil, representing "$975,000,000 WPA funds which FDR wants to spend at his discretion."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Republican elephant is split in half, with the front half supporting Willkie and the back half, representing GOP Congressmen, running away.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two men, representing the "political profits," count unhatched eggs in a barn. One of the eggs, Maine, has hatched a baby Republican elephant.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: John L. Lewis and the Republican elephant reach towards each other. Both have clothes pins on their noses.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Republican elephant and Democrat donkey are both hitched to a wagon representing the Nation's welfare.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Caricature of President Franklin Delano Roosevelt, smoking a cigarette.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Republican elephant sits, afraid, at a table for the old guard in a smoke-filled room.

did.unittitle: "All Right, Wendell, You Asked For It!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Presidential race as a boxing match. President Franklin Delano Roosevelt, as "The Champ," enters to ring to fight Republican candidate Wendell Willkie.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Presidential race as a boxing match. Republican candidate Wendell Willkie is on the ground, knocked out, after round one.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A Nazi soldier, representing Hitlerism, stands in a burning Europe with a body on the ground. From the United States, the arm of President Franklin Delano Roosevelt points at him accusingly.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An eagle, representing the United States, carries the President's message and a ship representing aid to Britain over the Atlantic.

did.unittitle: 'And you stay on the job too!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man representing Congress, examining his Adjournment Plans, is called back to work by a man representing workers implementing defence production plans.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two men composing music on the same piano, backs to each other. One is writing a piece entitled "ASCAP" (the American Society of Composers, Authors and Publishers) and the other "BMI" (Broadcast Music, Inc.). Both of them are performing rights organizations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt at a grave at night, carrying a shovel and pick ax, is surprised by a ghostly question mark representing the "Third Term Tradition."

did.unittitle: "You can't do this to me - I'm from the National Geographic!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three African tribesmen with spears carry a white man from National Geographic tied to a pole.

did.unittitle: "He's at that Stark Realism of His Again."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man writes on a typewriter, wearing only his boxer shorts and sitting on a box of beans. Two women talk in the doorway to the room, commenting that he is working on stark realism.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson golfing, hitting the ball over the obstacles of the Mexican situation, Wall Street opposition, and the Senate and landing it on the green (prosperity).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Figures representing Japan and the United States run a hurdles race. The hurdles start short and get taller for Japan, with the expanding power of the U.S. The hurdles start tall and get shorter for U.S., starting with the need to build the war machine and cross the ocean and ending with the expended strength of Japan. Japan is confident of winning but does not see that his hurdles will get taller at the end of the race.

did.unittitle: "But Making It Stick is Something Else"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hitler is trying to paste Nazi wallpaper on the ceiling, standing on scaffolding representing the Northern, Central, and Southern fronts. However, it is not sticking well on Leningrad, Moscow, Crimea, or the conquered countries.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several Democratic politicians thumb their noses at a statue of Thomas Jefferson, the father of the Democratic Party. The politicians include Hopkins, Ickey, Perkins, Wallace, Boss Kelly, Boss hague, and "Chi" Supt. of Sewers Garry.

did.unittitle: "Oh! So He's Decided to Run After All!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1940 presidential race as a football game. President Franklin Delano Roosevelt is the quarterback, with a front line made up of Wally and the hatchet men. Republican candidate Wendell Willkie breaks through the line and chases Roosevelt, who yells that he needs to go inspect his defenses.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A large figure representing Italy tries to catch a tiny figure representing Greece in a pair of pliers, but Greece is able to grab the ends and catch Italy instead.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam as a pony express rider, coming to the 1940 election station where he can change horses. He is riding a tired horse (Roosevelt and the New Deal), and a fresh horse (Willkie administration) is waiting. A man, Ickey, cautions Uncle Sam not to change to an inexperienced horse as there are dangerous years ahead.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two men, representing the Republican and Democrat parties, ride to meet President Franklin Delano Roosevelt, depicted as the Egyptian Sphinx, to ask the riddle about if he intends to run for a third term.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A woman, representing a third term for President Franklin Delano Roosevelt, pulls the petals off a daisy chanting "he loves me, he loves me not" to determine if he will run a third time.

did.unittitle: "Quenching His Thirst with a Slow Poison"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A large soldier with a bloody sword representing Japan drinks from a bottle of poison labeled "Manila Temporary Gains." The skeletal figure of death watches him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An old man, representing 1940, passes on the world to a startled baby representing 1941. 1940 is bandaged and his robe is full of bullet holes. The world has gun barrels pointing all around it.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hitler puts on the "boot" of Italy, telling a bandaged Mussolini "If you can't fill 'em no more - I can."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A tiny, battered Mussolini stands before Hitler, who says "My! How you have shrunk since the last time we met!! What's happened to you?"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A woman representing France is crushed under a Nazi boot in field of bones. Planes drop pieces of paper announcing that "The Yanks are Coming," causing her to smile.

did.unittitle: "Looks Like The Old Bird Means Business!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The American Eagle orders a vulture representing pro-Nazi activities to leave the United States.

did.unittitle: "Better Not Invite Anyone to a Fish Dinner Just Yet!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Democrat donkey fishes in a lake representing the campaign using a pole of New Deal hopes. However, he has hooked a frog of opposition to a third term instead.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The GOP Republican elephant eats a hearty meal at the campaign table made up of Vital Issues from Willkie's Coffeyville Address and drinks a cup of coffee "pep." With that energy, he says "Bring on your donkey! - Bring on a couple of donkeys!!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Italy boot, with Nazi spurs. The wounds from Italian reverses in Greece and Africa are bound with a bandage of Nazi victories.

did.unittitle: "There can be no division on this issue!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Republican elephant and Democrat donkey walk behind Uncle Sam. He carries the platforms of both parties, both of which include adequate national defense.

did.unittitle: "We've Really Got Our Dander Up Now!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An angry Uncle Sam, holding a rifle representing the war effort, strangles a rat representing Japan next to a cemetery with the fallen heroes of Bataan and Corregidor.

did.unittitle: "Christmas Seals Will Help Seal His Fate"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Grim Reaper, representing the Tuberculosis Toll in Delaware, urges people not to by Christmas Seals for his sake.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt is tied to a chair representing the Presidency for a third term by New Dealers and Ickes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Charles W. Hardesty of Delaware fishes in a lake of Works Relief Funds for a fish of $10,000,000 or more for his state.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Caricature of an African American man in a suit and top hat, asking for gin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson hypnotises Uncle Sam to make him believe that the Mexican policy is working, that arbitration only applies some of the time, and that military preparations are adequate.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson talks with former governor Cox about the nice day and the White House. The conversation gets tense when Cox asks about unemployment in Washington, which Wilson says is a touchy subject.

did.unittitle: "A Coalition Cabinet Big Energy for the National Crisis"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson seated at a long table with 10 men. The heads of the men are replaced by numbers.

did.unittitle: "To Mexico, England, Germany, France, Ireland or Patagonia"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson writes a generic letter to several countries (Mexico, England, Germany, France, Ireland, or Patagonia) condemning them for "rudeness" and promising a slap on the wrist if it does not stop. He also writes not to take the note too seriously, unless he changes his mind.

did.unittitle: "Delivering the Burden in the Solemn Referendum"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam arrives at the White House, leading a bandaged Democrat donkey which is carrying the unconscious figures of the Wilson Administration and the Wilson League.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Democrat donkey runs from Wilson enthusiasm, looking behind and not seeing the barbed wire of foreign entanglements in front of him. These entanglements are the result of following the Wilson League way.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two long rows of men wait to be called by President Woodrow Wilson to serve in his administration.

did.unittitle: "Mr. Wilson predicts that he will go to bed at 9 oclock election night."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In the first frame, Woodrow Wilson tells the publicity bureau that he will be going to bed at nine o'clock election night. The New Jersey State House, shaped like an octopus, is in the background. In the second frame, Wilson's staff has been buried by the Roosevelt Landslide and Wilson is carried off to a hospital bed.

did.unittitle: "The man who votes for Wilson will be asked to forget and forgive."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Six frames of school scenes with a progressively older student. In each, Woodrow Wilson is the teacher or professor, disciplining the student.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson as a school boy with his first report card. He got an "E" in all subjects: tariff, currency, income tax, civil service, appointments, suffrage, canal tolls, foreign affairs, tact, industry, deportment, and motives.

did.unittitle: "Cartoon of the Day: The Star Battery in Action and A Welcome as Warm as The Texas Sun"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In the first frame, Woodrow Wilson, as a baseball player, is at bat against pitcher Theodore Roosevelt. Roosevelt gets strike one while Wilson declares he will disregard him. In the second frame, "modest heroes home from the border" are hugged by a woman representing Chicago.

did.unittitle: "The name of Taft was not cheered during the first four days but when he was nominated, there was some labored cheering."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A crowd of men, delegates from several states, raise a hand to cheer Taft, but all are frowning, a few check their watches, and one is asleep.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The G.O.P. Republican Convention. In frame one, Democrats (Wilson, McAdoo, Palmer, Edwards, and Cox) wait outside the convention hall. In frame two, delegates within the convention wait outside the platform committee room. And in frame three, the candidates (including Theodore Roosevelt) and the general public wait outside the convention hall.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In the "old style method" the presidential message is read by a clerk and Congress is sparsely attended and no one is paying attention. In the "modern Wilsonian method" Wilson delivers his own message, Congress is full and everyone pays attention.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson, leaving a room full of "southern Democrats in government jobs," exclaims "How shocking! The Republicans are raising the issue of sectionalism!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man lectures to a full house at the opera.

did.unittitle: "President Wilson Hopes Peace will not be Declared before November"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson looks at two fortresses representing the Republican Convention and Progressive Convention.

did.unittitle: "In, the spring, a young man's fancy lightly turns to thoughts of love"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson calls the convention to order over the phone. The Democrat donkey offers flowers to the Bull Moose Progressive Party. The Republican elephant sees this angrily.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Justice Hughes leaves the Supreme Court to run for President. Images of many prominent previous Republican presidential candidates with beards are along the top. In the corner, Theodore Roosevelt promises Uncle Sam that he will not run on a third party ticket.

did.unittitle: "How the President can Help Protect, not only the Mexican border, but the whole country"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson calls the Texas, Arizona, and New Mexico militia to rise and defend themselves along the Mexican border. Uncle Sam tries to draw Wilson's attention to the Democratic Congress, which is reaching into the pork barrel behind the President's back while ignoring and stepping on preparedness measures. Wilson's style is depicted as "the harsh word and the small stick."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson shouts across the Atlantic to stop Kaiser Wilhelm II of Germany from attacking Great Britain with a U-Boat submarine, and consequently American commerce interests, in the name of humanity. Behind him is "5 Years of Mexican Chaos and Inhumanity." Figures representing bandits and revolutionists sit on a pile of rubble which is crushing both Americans and the Mexican people.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson depicted as the State's Attorney argues a case before Congress (the judge). Uncle Sam is the plaintiff and Germany is the defendant. Germany is charged with 65 counts of assualt with a deadly weapon. A submarine sits on the table next to the figure representing Germany.

did.unittitle: "Will They Collide or Stop at the Shrine"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson (on a horse, with a flag reading "Humanity in God We Trust") and Kaiser Wilhelm II of Germany (in a car representing German Submarine Methods and a flag reading "Self Preservation Gott Mit Umns") race towards each other on a road labeled war, heading for a collision. They are approaching an intersection with a road labeled peace. Christ, on the cross, is at the intersection.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson looks across the Atlantic, holding a stick of unpreparedness and a notice that American lives must not be jeopardized in the war zone by German submarines. He sees a ship with American's on board pull into a harbor in the war zone, facing mines, submarines, and fighting figures representing the blockade.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In the first frame, Asquith, the Prime Minister of Great Britain, is attacked by the army aristocracy and a lion. President Woodrow Wilson comments that he should have pursued a policy of watchful waiting rather than ordering the troops into Ulster. In the second frame, Pancho Villa rests after chasing the federal army from Torreon. Wilson comments that he should follow up on his advantage rather than adopting a policy of watchful waiting.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pancho Villa, on a stage behind the curtain for a production of the Battle of Torreon. His audience, made up of world leaders, is impatient for him to begin but he tells them to wait. At the same time, a Mexican general raises funds from Mexican millionaires to aid the president.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson as a school teacher. He tells his students, the Senate and the House, that they have worked hard for a year and can have a vacation as soon as they pass the anti-trust bills. Their hopes for vacation are dashed and they complain that they will be there all summer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson as a doctor performing surgery, cutting a man representing the principle of arbitration in half. Wilson explains to a shocked Uncle Sam that he thinks 50% of arbitration is enough and that the operation pleases the children, who represent the labor vote.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson gives repeated orders to Mexican general and president Huerta, which are ignored, that Huerta should not run for president, that the Congress was elected illegally, that he should salute the American flag he insulted, and that cargo of arms and ammunition on the ship Ypiranga must not be landed. The orders are watched by the leaders of England, France, Germany, and Japan, who are initially alarmed but fear no consequences by the end.

did.unittitle: "Mr. Wilson's Plan of Cleaning Up the Mexican Situation"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson sweeps a dog representing Mexican disorder under a bed with a lot of trash. The dog comes back out with several puppies. Wilson tries to be kind to them but is bitten by the mother and chases them back under the bed.

did.unittitle: "Latest Communique from the West Bank of the Mississippi"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scenes of Woodrow Wilson: getting a donkey to do tricks, his suprise at receiving the Democratic nomination for president, and receiving a "teacher of the world" medal from Senator Ollie James.

did.unittitle: "The Day's News from Different Points of View"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three frames of different people reading the same newspaper. Woodrow Wilson is unhappy to see an article on Hughes acceptance speech of the Republican nomination, as Hughes criticized Wilson's Mexico policy and there has been more death on the border. The Kaiser is unhappy because the articles are all about German loses. And an unidentified man enjoys the weather section, finding the news to be much more interesting than usual.

did.unittitle: "Some Educational Movies that Mr. Hughes Might Use"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Titles and scenes from three "movies" that show the failures of President Woodrow Wilson: American Prosperity is credited to the war rather than Wilson, Huerta and the Kaiser are credited with keeping the U.S. out of the war rather than Wilson, and the unresolved unrest in Mexico.

did.unittitle: "If he had backed through that first obstruction, he would have avoided all the others."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson's directing of the country is depicted as a train that keeps running into obstacles as he tries to take the easiest and safest way. The obstacles include the wheat shortage, rail strike threat, allies' blacklist, Villa, strict accountability, German submarine crisis no. 1, British mails seizers, Ypirange arms shipment, flag salute, and Huerta defiance.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson as the old woman who lived in a shoe, with many fighting children representing America's problems: submarine crisis, the Lusitania, British blacklist, Mexican conditions, flag salute fiasco, U.S. mails interference, unpreparedness conditions, and the Japanese crisis. An image above asks which symbol do you prefer: the Eagle ("first be sure you're right and then go ahead") or the crawfish (first be sure you write and then go ahead and write some more"). And image below shows that the Underwood Tariff hurt American industry but the war traffic has stimulated it, and wages have gone up since the war began but the purchasing power of the dollar has shrunk.

did.unittitle: "In the Hands of the Jury. Is he Pro or Anti-draught?"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson is in the jury box, deciding if beer will be prohibited or not. Uncle Sam waits for his decision, nervous.

did.unittitle: "Seeing the Wilson Party from a camouflaged observation post"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: When President Woodrow Wilson and his party are in the public eye, they are gentlemen in a carriage greeting the crowd. When unobserved, the party is riding military guns.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Woodrow Wilson tries to coax a performer, representing Italy, onto the stage as the audience gets angry, saying that the whole world is waiting. But Italy, encouraged by Lloyd George, will not until he gets what he wants.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A set of large footprints move in the direction of no entangling alliances for the United States. President Woodrow Wilson walks in the opposite directions of the tracks with legislation for Article X and entangling alliances under his arm.

did.unittitle: "Can You Blame Him for Wanting Reservations?"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson tries to get Uncles Sam to sign a contract for the League of Nations. Uncle Sam is hesitant, noting that the U.S. furnishes all the assets and Europe all the liabilities. Newspapers on the floor indicate that there is trouble in Germany, the Balkans, and Asia Minor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Woodrow Wilson insists that Italy must not have Fiume. In response, crowds in Italy destroy the "Via President Wilson" and cheer on a speaker denouncing him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: William Jennings Bryan boxes with a monkey before a huge crowd of spectators. The cartoon refers to Bryan's attacks on evolution.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A large hand, representing taxpayers, draws a line around a shocked small man representing a budget building, with an appropriations bill under his arm, and orders him to stay within the line. The line represents income.

did.unittitle: "I Never T'ot Little Oceans Ever Got Dis Rough!!!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mussolini jumps out of a warship about to be swamped by a wave representing Greece.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: German troops have run over a woman representing France in a tank and are driving off. Mussolini stands over her with a bloody sword.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt speeds through Connecticut on the "Third-Term Special" train, using a telescope to see the area.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wendell Willkie rides triumphantly on the GOP Republican elephant, with Governor Baldwin leading him.

did.unittitle: "Even If He Jumps All The Way Over He'll Land in a Hole"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man, W.B.Y., tries to jump over barrels representing years for a $2,100,000 Bond Issue (1942-1953), but at the end of the barrels is a hole representing deferred payments.

did.unittitle: "Now that the smiling mask of diplomatic friendship has been removed, it is our job to corner and exterminate the rat!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam removes a smiling mask to reveal a rat, representing Japan wanting war. U.S. warships with "Remember Hawaii" on their guns invade.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several scenes related to the production of navy ships being behind schedules. Men in the navy try to encourage workers to do their share.

did.unittitle: "With a Gift of Security to the Children in America"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Santa Claus, with a bag of National Defense instead of toys, fills the stockings of the Air Force with a plane, Army with a soldier, and Navy with a boat. Also visible in his bag are men representing the draft, reserves, and national guard.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various scenes relating sporting events to the war.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two boats are at the dock of "8 long years," ready to cross the lake of "present crisis" to arrive at "peace and prosperity." One boat is a power boat in good condition named "Super Special" and piloted by Wendell Willkie. The other is a leaking rowboat named "New Deal" piloted by Franklin Delano Roosevelt.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two columns of the White House have been damaged by the first and second term. A third column is the "last remaining guarantee of American Democracy." A man uses "G.O.P. Life-Saver Spray" to kill New Deal "bugs" heading for the third column.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with white tempera, 15 3/4 x 11

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam contemplating problem on chalkboard that looks as follows: T.R. (Teddy Roosevelt) + ME = I, I + Presidency = X, X = ?.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man in a top hat.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man leaves a U.S. ship that has arrived in another country (the flag includes the British flag) with a bag of money in each hand.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man holding a bouquet looks at a boy sitting in a field next to similar flowers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man sits at a desk, writing with a quill pen.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man seated, wearing a laurel wreath, holding a scroll in one hand and a book in the other.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man, R.R.

did.unittitle: "Chas M. Schwab Erected by a Grateful People"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statue of Charles Schwab at the crossroads of four streets named for him.

did.unittitle: "One Czar who Never Called Peace Congress"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A fat man with a gavel.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Beautiful young woman and a crowned gentleman greeting money bag on table. The bag is labeled "Papa's money." On back: Three ladies walking on a street.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several men dressed as villains with rapiers, waiting for innocent Miss Democracy as she comes down the stairs. The men are Wilson, Harmon, Dix, Gaynor, Clark, Underwood, and Bryan.

physdesc.physfacet: Two-sided: pen and ink, lithograph, 17 1/2 x 20 3/4

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side one: Well-to-do family scene on tennis court. Side two: In a locker room, a man rubs "Esopus Oil" on the leg of another man. In the background is a "$40,000,000 Safe and Sane".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Old couple, man fat, wife lean, he is bringing dessert in, she graciously refuses.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man holds a bag labeled "Paper Trust."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cleveland being brought in over city by a stork.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Confrontation of two gentlemen in front hall of great house.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A confused man in a suit stands among the stumps of several trees, counting on his fingers. In the background, anther man looks out the window of his log cabin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man shovels coal and laughs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Judge George Gray of Delaware as ripe peach suspended over basket, presumably referring to some political nomination in the wind.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cleveland and Bayard battering "Republican Wall" with the ram of "Sound Democratic Principles."

did.unittitle: "Donts or How To Be a Gentleman and a Governor"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man reads from a very large book titled "Donts or How to be a Gentleman and a Governor."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man wearing a crown, in fancy dress with a rapier in both hands.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man with a very large nose, wearing a monocle.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A balding man holds a tape measure.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man with a key in one hand and his fist on a capitol building.

did.unittitle: "One Man Who Thinks the Gerry Society is All Right"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An old man in a sailor suit, with Electra on his hat, dances a jig while a young boy watches surprised.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A black man with a simple sword. Next to him is a fancy vest and sword that are the reward "for a good boy."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man with a large white beard.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A fat young man, thumbs in his fancy vest.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A balding man with a high collar pulls wires loose from a radio.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man with wild hair and glasses.

did.unittitle: "Adlai E. Stevenson, Vice President under Cleveland"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man with a large bow tie.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man with a winged lion on his hat, a very large mustache, and an arm full of package from Paris.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A younger man with a large head.

did.unittitle: "Henry H. Vreeland, President of Met. St. R.R."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man pointing to a New York train cars moving in a circle labeled "perpetual motion."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man pushes open the U.S. Senate door.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man with a large mustache and a book under his arm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man hiding indictments behind his back.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man opening declined invitations from many lords and ladies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A determined man marches along the Nicaragua Canal.

did.unittitle: "Hugh J. Grant, Mayor of New York City"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man with a large beard who has just chopped down a pole.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with white tempera, 14 x 16 3/4

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Shadow of "Archboldism" floating over floor of Congress.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with blue pencil

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black showbusiness couple.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man holding a gavel for the New York City Council.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man shaped like an ox. There is a Standard Oil can on one horn and a rag with dollar signs on the other.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man shaped like an ostrich, with two other copies of his head as eggs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man depicted as a bird.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Politicians Parker, William Jennings Bryan, Gray, and Johnson set off fireworks on the Fourth of July.

did.unittitle: "How Can We Help Being Thankful? - Just look at This Partial List of Our Manifold Blessings!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scenes of problems in the country painted as blessings: the American tin industry, prohibition, two escaped corrupt politicians, the large number of pensioners, "Maggie Murphy's Home" and the number of candidates for political office. Also includes the genuine blessings of "good old customs" (a couple meeting, getting married, and having a baby) and that the country is safe.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scenes of the failure of a livery stable, saloon, and American tailors, and of the success of Hustler's Magazine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Puck's" cherubic symbol out and about in the world. Six scenes including last of retreat back into "Puck Offices."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Republican politician James G. Blaine atop chimney with numerous lightening rods attached to him to attract nearby bolts of "Republican Presidential Nomination." the rods include "vigorous foreign policy," "reciprocity," and several references to his grandfather.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wendell Willkie as a cowboy, branding the Democrat donkey with a Nazi swastika and condemning the party machine. He also inadvertently brands the Republican elephant, his own party, as well.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Democrat donkey as the Trojan horse entering the Republican Camp, with Republican candidate Wendell Willkie emerging from inside with "bungling campaign tactics."

did.unittitle: "Old Guard Politics in a New Role"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man in a chauffeur uniform tries to convince Wendell Willkie that he needs a regular driver. His current driver, Johnie Q Willkie Fan, looks on in dismay, sitting in the car 'Willkie Boom."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wendell Willkie "power personality" is depicted as a large lamp post, but his campaign as a tiny candle.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A crowd of people, representing popular demand for Wendell Willkie, parachute onto "the tight little island of Boss Control," possibly New York City.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Republican elephant puffs from his trunk both a hot candidate and a cold platform at the G.O.P. convention.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The 1940 Chicago Democratic Convention, depicted as an empty 3-ring circus tent.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam smiles and listens to the sounds coming from Elwood, Indiana.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man, representing the election year, looks at the capitol. He has defense plans under his arm and a bottle of "old bust-head politics" in his hands.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The G.O.P., represented as a chicken with elephant legs, sits on a nest representing the Republican Philadelphian Convention and says "I've just got to lay an egg!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A Congressman with two personalities. The Patriotic side wants to pass the draft bill, while the Political side cautions himself to wait until after the election.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A woman, representing "inert leadership" is distracted by the political convention and political ballyhoo. She ignores her war knitting. A newspaper has the headline "England Stands Alone."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The G.O.P. Republican elephant trapped in barbed wire representing their defense record.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam holds onto the tail of an angry bull representing the unprotected watershed of the Mississippi River.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wendell Willkie as a football player running in a circle, gaining yards with "Anti-F.D.R. Tirade" but losing the same number with "New Deal Endorsement." The Republican elephant watches from the stands, confused.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Republican elephant and Democrat donkey work together to forge a sword (Defense) on an anvil representing unity.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wendell Willkie as a trapeze artist, swinging between several trapezes representing issues: utilities, Wallace farm plan, lower taxes, Lewis support, more aid for Britain, more foreign trade, Girdler support, Argentine beef, isolation, more relief, and old G.O.P. farm aid. The Republican elephant, chained to a "Wall Street Peg," watches, impressed.

did.unittitle: "Not All Black, But They Stand Out"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A line of sheep, representing the union labor movement. About a quarter of the sheep are black. Three of the black sheep are labeled: unworthy leader, racketeer leader, and selfish leader.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Republican elephant wearing a paper hat representing the Anti-Third Term Press fires a pop gun representing the Third Term Issue. Behind him, tanks and airplanes represent the big issues of today.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wendell Willkie, Republican presidential candidate, says he will share the "New Deal Platform" with the President (Franklin Delano Roosevelt).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dewey rides on the Republican elephant's trunk, with the elephant having trouble keeping the pace.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Presidential candidate Wendell Willkie rides four horses at once on an "anti-third term" road. The horses representing Aid to Britain, Wall Street, C.I.O. Lewis,a nd Isolation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam and another figure, possibly Great Britain, sign a reciprocal trade agreement, while soldiers representing the German-Italy-Japan Pact march in the background.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A Nazi makes a bloody hand print, representing the murder of hostages, in a book on the New Order in France.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Goebbels, Von Ribbentrop, and Hitler as three witches making a poison in a cauldron representing Yugoslavia.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam stands on a low rock, about to be flooded, and reaches for higher ground. There are progressively higher rocks representing strategic bases, U.S.-Canadian joint defense, and destroyer help to Britain.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Halifax, depicted as the anti-Christ, slays a man representing liberty and breaks the Christian cross to make it into a Nazi swastika.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hitler stands on the end of a gun barrel representing the Axis, preparing to stab the world with his sword. Molotoff stands with him, wringing his hands.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Hudson County Poll Books, smoking with scandal, are tied to the tail of the Democrat donkey.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man injects "constructive opposition program" into the back of a smiling Republican elephant.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man with a sword stands on a pedestal with his foot on top of burning poll books. An inscription on the pedestal reads "Democratic Candidate for Governor Cannot be Too Strong. In Condemnation of Fraud and Corruption in the Conduct of Elections."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man representing Labor and a man representing management each put a cat into a large bag labeled "Kilkenny Cat Stuff."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The presidential race between Franklin Delano Roosevelt and Wendell Willkie. Roosevelt is the Democrat donkey, running on stilts with his nose in the air (a "high horse") as a "non-political" candidate. Willkie is the Republican elephant, running along the ground as a "strictly political" candidate.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt and another man in Roman togas. The other man pulls the "third term" laurel wreath from Roosevelt's head.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man representing the West holds a small politician in his hand about to start a 7200 mile campaign tour.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A donkey, representing the Democratic National Committee listens to a gramophone playing "third term agitation," but does not understand it.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man and woman speak together at a desk. The caption reads: Can't the case of Van Swell against Van Swell be put off? I'm afraid not my dear. Are you -er- their daughter? No. I'm the correspondent and if the case is tried now, the papers won't be able to give us any space!

did.unittitle: "Modern School Boy - Teacher, would you mind telling my chauffeur I'm kept in an' he'll have to wait?"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A boy being kept after school by his teacher. He asks the teacher to tell his chauffeur that he will have to wait.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black kettle labeled "Albany Legislature" and black pot labeled "Insurance Corruption" floating down stream of Graft together.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Adolph Hitler in a wizard robe and hat, Nazi swastika's circling his head and a book entitled "Mein Kampf" in his hand. The caption reads: Astrological Adolf. Your cosmic punch is on der fritz, your Russian hunch der bunk, you yell vot's happened to mein Blitz? Adolph - you're plain hunch drunk!

did.unittitle: "But It's All Greek to Me, Professor"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Adolph Hitler as a teacher at the Nazi War College, trying to get a confused man to understand the Blitz Krieg Technique.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Japanese Emperor Hirohito with a smoking foot caused by the Philippines.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Abraham Lincoln, standing at Pearl Harbor, captioned with portions of his Gettysburg Address.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A starving, exhausting horse representing the farm relief problem is hitched outside of the "General Campaign Issues Store."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Papa New Deal" looks at triplets representing 1932, 1936, and 1940 and dreams of quadruplets, quintuples, and sextuplets. He says "I'll Make Papa Kionne Look Like a Piker!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A woman, representing New Deal bureaucracy, wants to teach Uncle Sam the three step. His feet hurt from shoes representing 1932 and 1936.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt has Ickes, city bosses, and Wallace as puppets on strings and makes them dance.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A skeleton pours wine made in 1941 into bottles from 1917 and 1918.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two fat straw men fight each other with wooden swords.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Franklin Delano Roosevelt points to Japan on a globe with his pencil. The glob has "Global War" written over Russia.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Republican candidate Wendell Willkie jumps onto the Franklin Delano Roosevelt bandwagon instead. The G.O.P. bandwagon is crashed in the background.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A soldier representing Japan tries to close a gate over the Pacific sea lanes, while a man representing Great Britain stands in Singapore, holding it open so that ships can get through.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: William Randolph Hearst as a puppeteer causes Champt Clark and Brisbane to sling mudd at another man.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Democrat Party donkey and Republican Party elephant meet each other looking for loopholes in the Hatch Act regulating national political activities.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Republican Party elephant knocks on the door of Wendell Willkie's campaign headquarters with a newspaper announcing that Willkie is a liberal democrat and supports the New Deal behind his back.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Republican Party elephant balances on an electrical wire, holding a pole to try to keep its balance. On one end of the pole is Senator McNary, who believes the government should control electrical power (public ownership). On the other end of the pole is Wendell Willkie and the Power Trust, who believe it should be privately owned.

did.unittitle: "Somebody'll Have to Put the Cat Out!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A black cat representing the private vs. public power issue stands at the foot of a bed where Wendell Willkie, Senator McNary, and the Power Trust are sleeping.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Republican Party elephant stands on the end of a diving board representing the foreign policy plank, afraid to jump into the waters of popular sentiment.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A witch flies over a town with the banner "Willkie or Ruin" attached to her broomstick.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Compares the offices of the President of the United States (Franklin Delano Roosevelt) and the President of the Commonwealth Southern Corp (Wendell Willkie). Roosevelt's office is full of books of successful New Deal and relief programs. Willkie's office has a trash can filled with tape or wires.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam writes on a casualty list from Pearl Harbor, Wake Island, and Manila "Resolved - That These Shall be Avenged!"

did.unittitle: "Oh Boy, Have I Got Him on the Run!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wendell Willkie runs with a sign reading "Only I Can Do Better," thinking he is ahead but in truth he is running on the "coattails" of the New Deal.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers representing the facts are thrown out of the Wendell Willkie train as excess baggage.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wendell Willkie, standing on top of a giant I, pledges to bring about a new world on his own. Figures representing Congress, the people, and the Republican party say together "I guess we don't figure in his plans?"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hitler, Mussolini, and Hirohito with their Axis plans for remaking the world are surprised to see Wendell Willkie's claim that he will bring a new world.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Republican Party elephant as a hobo watches the "Wendell Willkie Campaign Tour Special" fly by. A newspaper indicates that Willkie has switched from Tammany Democrat to a Liberal Democrat and supports the New Deal.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with blue pencil

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paris Exposition and Chicago World's Fair take backseat to Pan American Exposition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three drunken Yalies wave down the street as elder man and grinning young man look on.

did.unittitle: "The Inside Situation in Tammany Hall in 1903",

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with blue pencil

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tammany tiger in Salvationist clothes spreads "City Reform War Cry" in bars.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wide-eyed owl on branch.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Metamorphosis in five panels of boy turning into a green apple after eating one.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Human-faced meteorite streaks across night sky as family rushes from farmhouse.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A baseball cartoon. The Indians team is represented as a boat, captained by Fohl, which is sinking due to damage caused by a storm and a Yankees shark.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three women archers shoot at a target, all hitting the bull's eye. A cherub watches and winks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam, with a note of Equal Rights to All Nations, interrupts two men sitting in Europe, one holding the "Yap Mandate Grab" and the other the "Mesopotamian Oil Grab Mandate."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A beaten man representing Germany is sentenced in an international peace court by President Clemenceau and ordered to surrender land, pay five billion dollars, and give up his army and navy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Events expected in 1932: Japan will pursue Manchuria, the U.S. presidential campaign (Ritchie, Baker, Roosevelt, and Al Smith), and Europe will expect further money from the U.S.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several scenes about the inconvenient weight of the silver dollar.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several scenes of presidential candidate Governor Alfred E. Smith speaking to his supporters in New York and Boston, criticizing the Republican Party and his opponent Herbert Hoover.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson, holding a stick representing the Army and Navy, throws Huerta out of Mexico.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson holds a School of Etiquette to convince former presidents William Howard Taft and Theodore Roosevelt to stop criticizing him. Taft and Roosevelt fight initially but by the end sing "For He's a Jolly Good Fellow."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam plays the drums and brings a man representing Congress running to sign President Woodrow Wilson's U.S. Rights Recruits document. William Jennings Bryan watches, surprised, from behind a wall.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson is supported by former presidents William Howard Taft and Theodore Roosevelt after the resignation of Wilson's secretary of state William Jennings Bryan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam lies sick in bed (representing Law and Order) after drinking bottles of crooked enforcement, poison stills, rum bootlegging fleets and court in-decisions. President Woodrow Wilson prescribes that he drink light wines and beer instead.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Einstein solves several policy and social problems: U.S. taxation, solar powered cars, bringing the dollar back to normalcy, tobaccoless smoke, and foodless meals.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several images of Wilson as Governor of New Jersey.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scenes of President Woodrow Wilson being distracted from his duties by his new grandson: writing fewer messages to Congress, interrupting peace conferences to attend to the child, and telling stories about Sayre during cabinet meetings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man begs for the chance to campaign for president but is denied. Flyers for Hughes and Wilson are on the ground.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Albert Einstein coming to Boston and finding his theories do not apply to daily social life.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson and his 14 Points of Peace is saluted by ships from several countries.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson teaching his Fourteen Amendments at the Versailles Peace Conference.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man divorces his wife for being a nagging back seat driver. He refers to driving as steering the wheels of justice.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several scenes of individuals and families changing their names after a New York Court ruled names could be changed to anything. The poor and immigrants change their names to higher-status names, and a bootlegger changes his name to protect himself.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Statue of Liberty shines her light of freedom on the ship carrying the U.S. peace delegation, including President Woodrow Wilson with his 14 peace principles, to the peace conference in 1918.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A woman, representing Massachusetts, sets up a summer home for President Woodrow Wilson. The summer homes for presidents Cleveland and Taft, also in Massachusetts, are depicted in the backgrounds.

did.unittitle: "Wilson Campaign Fund to be Contributed by the People."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A working man, small business man, and housewife contribute to Woodrow Wilson's campaign fund.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hoover and Curtis ride the Republican Party elephant, whose front half has become a camel, struggling into the "Eastern Modification Sentiment" wind.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson golfing on vacation. He refuses to take calls regarding the German note and Mexico, but leaves as soon as he receives a call that his grandchild wants him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Memorial to Woodrow Wilson, showing his place in the history books as educator, governor, World War president and world peace president.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson plays golf while various groups comment on his style (Japanese spies, Wall St. scouts, office seekers, an official guide, and William Jennings Bryan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Woodrow Wilson at the head of the parade to his presidential inauguration. Behind him are William Jennings Bryan, Princeton professors, Princeton Cottage Club, Murphy representing the Tammany Democrats, the "In Bad Club," "The Original Wilson Man - 50000 Strong," office seekers and suffragettes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A gold bond investor dreams of making billions in devaluation profits, but is awakened by a Supreme Court Justice ruling that he cannot do that.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: V. Huerta, seated in a Dictator chair, packs his bags and prepares to leave Mexico City to avoid revolutionary Pancho Villa.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
