scopecontent.p: Several scenes of President Woodrow Wilson speaking.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Woodrow Wilson chases the Progressive Party Bull Moose and Republican Party Elephant from Massachusetts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An old man (1915) coaches the new year (1916) on how to survive by minding his P's: peace, prosperity, preparedness, presidency, and proposals.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt sits on a tired Democratic Party donkey on the road to a 3rd term as chairman Edward J. Flynn lights a fire under him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A disastrous kitchen, representing the National Food Problem, with too many cooks: farm blocks, black markets, OPA, Congress, and government agencies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt, depicted as Santa Claus, walks on a road representing fruitless New Deal experiements, heading towards $45,000,000,000 public debt. He is unconcerned, but the weight of the debt is carried by a laboring tax payer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: John L. Lewis runs to get on the "Democrats for Willkie" bandwagon, leaving a surprised Franklin Delano Roosevelt behind him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Franklin Delano Roosevelt addressing the crowd during presidential campaigns. In 1932 and 1936 it was "my friends." In 1940 it would be "my subjects" and in 1944 "my slaves."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt makes a wooden boy with his face representing the 3rd term, hoping it will come to life.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A line of donkeys jumps off a diving board into water.

did.unittitle: "Gee, I'm Lucky There's Two L's in Willkie!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man in a scholar's robe and hat changes the street sign of "Wall St." to "Willkie St."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man and the Republican Party elephant ride on a book, "Go West Young Man" by Horace Greeley.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Grave stones with the date and location of battles for liberty, and a quote by Coltan that a people must raise themselves to liberty to earn it, rather than being given it.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man, representing the Allies, uses a cross bow to try to shoot an apple, representing the invasion point, off the head of a worried man.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A plane, representing a third term for President Franklin Delano Roosevelt, attacks the Statue of Liberty.

did.unittitle: "The Farther He Stretches the More Vulnerable His Tail"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Japan, represented as a snake, reaches across the Pacific Ocean through the Philippines, Indies, and Singapore towards Australia.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt rides the Democrat Party donkey, coaxing it along a path to a third term with "more abundant life, socialistic schemes, and rosy promises." However, the donkey is distracted and horrified by a note that indicates debt is $45,000,000,000 and stops moving.

did.unittitle: "St. Michael's Spire - A Symbol of Survival"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: St. Michael's Spire stands undamaged while all the buildings around it have been destroyed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two huge hands holding whip labeled "Administrative Lawmaking" and purse labeled "Rewards from the Public Purse."

did.unittitle: "A Farmer Knows What a Farmer Needs",

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Shows "Free American Farmer" driving plow of "McNary Way." Refers to Senator Charles McNary's (R. Oregon) farm aid measures.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Shows a dachshund with spiked collar slobbering his water outside doghouse with swastika. Refers to Nazi electoral victory in Memel territory subsequent to surrender of area.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Shows baby of inflation in "New Deal cradle of fiscal fallacies" screaming "More!" and FDR as nurse feeding it its "unbalanced rations."

did.unittitle: "Sometimes It's a Good Idea to Change"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Shows Republican Presidential Candidate Wendell Willkie (1940) offering Uncle Sam, who until now has been riding the "Gnu Deal" (New Deal) with FDR, a ride on the Republican elephant because FDR's Gnu is about to drown.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Shows chained figure labeled "Chief of State" held in palm of huge hand, probably Hitler's. Swastika looms in background. Figure is probably Neville Chamberlain referring to fall of Verdun.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An amateur strategist suggests that the way to stop the Japanese invasions throughout southeast Asia is for American forces to cut their supply lines.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A soldier representing Japan takes a huge spoon of sugar, representing Japanese conquests, to put in his cup of Java.

did.unittitle: "Now We Must Give Him the Tools"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A hand, representing the U.S., gives guns and planes and tanks and ships to General MacArthur, stationed in Australia, who is preparing to fight Japan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Adolf Hitler losing at cards to father time, using a globe and a history book for a table. Hitler lost a large bet for final victory in 1940, a smaller one for final victory in 1941, and now has a much smaller bet to make for final victory in 1942.

did.unittitle: "This is No Time to Pluck the Eagle"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam watches, horrified, as War Industry Labor Unions with excess demands and profiteering industries with huge profits pull most of the feathers off of an eagle representing U.S. War Emergency.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of George Washington. The caption reads: George Washington's Advice - No Third Term, No Foreign Entanglements."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A trash can labeled "Limbo of Forgotten Things," which contains a sign reading "it can't happen here," an umbrella labeled appeasement, and a calendar turned to December 1941.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A figure representing U.S. Tax Payers goes to work at a Power House, providing a 56 Billion Dollar War Budget to Defense Industries.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man, representing "we the people," sits in a classroom with War for a teacher. He says "All my life up to now Singapore, Malaya and the Dutch East Indies have been just far away places on the map, but I know better now! They're the places where rubber and tin come from!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An ordinary U.S. citizen sits in an arm chair, convinced that the United States will win World War II. Uncle Sam sits on a club representing vast resources and with a large back of inexhaustible wealth. They are about to be attacked from behind with a bolt of lightening representing the Axis Blitzkrieg tactics.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Republican Party elephant and Democrat Party donkey pull a plow together, directed by Uncle Sam, to create New Armament Plants on the field of National Defense.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man with a wig, representing "no third term tradition" is kicked out of the White House, probably by President Franklin Delano Roosevelt.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt driving a long fire truck representing national unity. The Republican Party elephant, representing the Loyal Opposition, drives the back portion of the truck.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ordinary citizens fight with each other over the Presidential Campaign of 1940. After the election, they shake hands and part ways, only to return to fight again at the next election in 1944.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An old man, writing in a history book, observes the people voting in the U.S. Presidential Election of 1940.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: John Q. Citizen, in bed on the presidential election night 1940, counts Democrat Party donkeys and Republican Party elephants instead of sheep to try to sleep.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Together, the Republican Party elephant and Democrat Party donkey throw a cat representing politics into a lack with a rock tied around its neck. But the elephant protests when the donkey puts a rock around it and wants it to jump into the lake as well.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Republican Party elephant tries to get a man representing "we the people" to enter the G.O.P. National Convention in 1940, represented as a circus tent. He is distracted by a train carrying "Roosevelt's Speech-Making Tour of the Nation, Non-Political of Course."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Presidential candidate Dewey as the Pied Piper, leading Mid-West primaries vote away from candidate Vandenberg.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An exhausted Uncle Sam reads in the newspaper 'Roosevelt Says He is Tired of the Third Term Question."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Political candidates try to do things against their nature to win votes: a candidate takes a fake picture of himself catching a fish to win votes in Florida, a candidate from the East tries to be a cowboy to win votes in the West, and a candidate from the city tries to milk a cow to win votes in the farm belt.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A woman, representing the Hatch "Clean Politics" Bill tries to wash a boy representing politics, who complains "I don't wanta be too clean!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The presidential election of 1940 is represented as a draft. Wendell Willkie has No. 1, because it would be his first term, and Franklin Delano Roosevelt has No. 3. A blindfolded man representing the American people reaches into a bucket to select one of them.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wendell Willkie dressed as a witch flies on a broomstick labeled Gallup Poll. The Democrat Party donkey looks up, frightened.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man, representing Senators and Representatives Up for Re-Election" sits on the steps of Washington, forgotten because of the 1940 Presidential Election race.

did.unittitle: "In the Bag and on the Fence"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Voters are represented as birds and presidential candidates Franklin Delano Roosevelt and Wendell Willkie as hunters. Each have "bagged" some loyal supporters, but it is in the independent voter that they spend the most time hunting.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man representing Washington Politics sits in a school classroom. He pretends to be reading the "National Defense Program" but is really reading "How to Get Re-Elected in November."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt as two people, one the President reading up on National Defense Problems, and the other the Candidate for Re-Election. Wendell Willkie challenges him to a joint debate on campaign issues but is told that "we are too busy to make political speeches!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt in Roman robes receives a crown for his third term from a kneeling Democrat Party donkey.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man, Al Smith Bolt, walks from New York to Elwood, Indiana, the home of Wendell Willkie.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wendell Willkie as a young boy riding the Republican Party elephant tries to get the elephant to break its way through a brick wall representing the "Solid South."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Republican Party elephant tries to row up a waterfall representing current world conditions during the 1940 Presidential Campaign, saying "And I thought I was going to have smooth sailing this trip!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt participates in the Democratic National Convention in Chicago over long distance direct wire from Washington.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Republican Party elephant runs to the National Convention in Philadelphia with a drum representing the Wendell Willkie Dark Horse Boom tied to his tail. The elephant runs from the noise, but his running actually causes it to boom more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The free spirit of England denies Hitler and the free spirit of America denies Roosevelt a third term.

did.unittitle: "After Big Game In the Red Sea"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt on the "ship of state" tries to catch a whale representing the third presidential term with the help of many supporters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wendell Willkie and Harry Hopkins meet each other in London and ask "what are you doing over here?"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Adolf Hitler and Mussolini as witches prepare trouble for the Balkans.

did.unittitle: "At this stage of a war he usually fights well"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Britain, represented by a lion, is attacked by arrows representing the invasion of low countries, the Norwegian campaign, and German Air Power, is angry and ready to fight.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A figure representing War forges National Unity before creating weapons.

did.unittitle: "The Greater the Storm the Greater the Flag Stiffens Out"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An American soldier walks through a thunderstorm, carrying the American flag.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Harry S. Truman is sworn in by Uncle Sam, his hands on the Holy Bible and a ray of light from the clouds hitting him. Uncle Sam says "- and may He guide you."

did.unittitle: "Even an Octopus Ought to Have Only Eight Arms"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam runs from an octopus representing the New Deal shouting "Eight is enough!" The arms of the octopus represent the years 1933-1940.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: France chained to post surrounded by skulls with smoldering city in background.

did.unittitle: "Persistent. He won't be happy til he gets it."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: He is Stalin sitting in bathtub waiting for "Balkan Blood Bath."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hitler holding Atlas at gunpoint and demanding the world.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon, 16 1/4 x 14 1/2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bum of the "Silk shirt era" of the last war advising "War Boom worker" to save his money instead of throwing it away as he did.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Adolf Hitler, depicted as an ape, escapes from a cage in a circus to attack England.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A tired, wounded Nazi soldier representing the Nazi Army on the French Coast prepares to put on boots labeled "Frist Phast of that Blitzkreig" and "That Second Boot." A man representing the British Isles lies in bed, reading a paper.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: John Bull struggles to hold Hitler (represented as a giant ape) back and keep him from attacking. The rope was given to him by Uncle Sam, but he is distracted from helping use the rope by the 1940 election. Small monkeys representing Mussolini and Japan are in the tree and a wolf representing Joseph Stalin stands in the background.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rich Americans eat and dance while a man representing John Bull's Navy tries to keep Hitler and his forces from entering the club.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam walks across the Atlantic Ocean with aid for Britain: food, clothing, medicine, armaments, and planes. He walks on rocks labeled "short of war" to stay above water, but as the rocks become shorter he comes close to drowning.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hitler stands on the edge of a cliff, holding a noose representing invasion, trapped by the simultaneous attacks of Russia, Libia, England, and sabotage in Germany.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A medical examiner for the draft looks at an out-of-shape man who has spent too much time at baseball games.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam walks through "vichy waters", fending off mosquitoes representing the Japanese "double cross" and stepping over a crocodile representing the French Fleet "double cross?" to get to an olive branch tree.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam and a woman, possibly Liberty, are welcomed by a man representing Japan, while Japanese soldiers, Hitler, and Mussolini hide in a cherry tree, preparing to attack.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man, representing general statistic figures and carrying a flag reading "potential U.S. Wealth" is bucked by a horse ("high horse attitude") that is startled by the attack on Pearl Harbor. He lands on a horse ("horse sense facts") with hooves labeled "Army, Navy, Air, Folks."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man representing Japan stands on a rope bridge connecting Japan with the United States. Japan is a exploding behind him, and he damages the bridge as he is attacked by an eagle. The bridge reads "conquer or die."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Political boxing match with Uncle Sam as referee.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon on pebbleboard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Trojan Horse labeled "3rd Term (FDR's)" sits outside of door of "Our American Liberty."

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon on pebbleboard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: He is Wendell Willkie, G.O.P. elephant complains that Willkie "up and left me with twins." Twins are Willkie-ites and Anti-Willkie-ites. A former Democrat, Willkie ran as the Republican Presidential candidate in 1940, lost to FDR, but became the Presidential personal representative (1941-42) to Europe.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon on pebbleboard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mussolini running in two directions at once and going nowhere.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon on pebbleboard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Republican Presidential candidate Wendell Willkie crashing through FDR's line in political football game.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon on pebbleboard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Banner-carrying people marching on Washington. Refers to dispute over FDR's running for a third term. (1940)

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon on pebbleboard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Critical view of FDR's record on unemployment for his first two terms.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon on pebbleboard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Big stick of national defense chopped up by New Deal academicians.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon on pebbleboard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Critical view on FDR's record on public spending for his first two terms.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon on pebbleboard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Shadow of 9,600,000 unemployed cast over the White House.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon on pebbleboard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: FDR and Henry Wallace hurry to attach more lightening rods to roof of New Deal barn before "No Third Term" storm strikes.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon on pebbleboard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Smoke rises out of Maine forests saying "No Third Term." Refers to FDR's running for a third term in 1940.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon on pebbleboard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hitler and Mussolini race to cover various crises within Axis countries.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon on pebbleboard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: FDR standing over auditorium in Chicago where Democratic Presidential Convention was held in 1940 pulling strings while saying, "I have no wish to be a candidate again."

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon on pebbleboard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hitler and Mussolini contemplating "Axis Peace Terms" in form of guillotine.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon on pebbleboard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam and Hitler yelling through megaphones at each other while FDR chases voter with salt shaker.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon on pebbleboard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Molotov blocking entrance to Stalin's office saying "Joe is NOT in."

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon on pebbleboard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Stalin knee deep in Danube changing sign from "Blue Danube" to "Red Danube."

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon on pebbleboard.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Truman trying to spur the rundown Democratic Donkey onward.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon on pebbleboard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Loaf of bologna labeled "Truman's continued attack on 80th Congress," with a few slices cut off.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon on pebbleboard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Harry Truman keeping watch on "Federal Employee Loyalty Data" with "Executive Authority" as Congress cries out for a "Red Spy Probe."

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon on pebbleboard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dove of Peace frozen in ice block of Cold War.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon on pebbleboard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Camp of Republican Presidential Candidate (1940), Wendell Willkie, with tents for campaign notables.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon on pebbleboard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Congress resting easy on cloud of Retirement Program while "hourly wage earners" and "$21 per month soldier" carry on below.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon on pebbleboard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Americans holding out on Midway Isle during WWII.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon on pebbleboard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Attorney General Biddle's "Official Secrets Bill" about to serve as gag for freedom of the press.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon on pebbleboard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: German officers complaining to Hitler about Britain's entrance into WWII.

physdesc.physfacet: Pen and ink with black crayon on pebbleboard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bewildered FDR reads in Boston paper what he said on July 30, 1932 about Budgets and Bankruptcy.

physdesc.physfacet: Crayon and ink on scratchboard, 15 x 20

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Santa "Pinchot" stands grinning before enormous "barrel" stocking labeled "$222,000,000.000 Departmental Estimates" while sack slung over his shoulder contains "$90,000,000.00 Budget."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Richly dressed figures labeled "Bath House John, Martin, Oberndorf, Powers, Kunz, Wulff, and Novak" are chased from lawn of "Franchise Grants" by policemen labeled "Independent Voter."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Beach scene in which man and woman recline beneath an umbrella while children play behind them.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Beautiful girl seated on couch looks admiringly at dignified gentleman kneeling beside her. Taylor was famous for his "Taylor-Made Girl" and influenced Charles Dana Gibson in his work.

did.unittitle: "To Keep Madness and Misery Out of the New World.",

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Shows Uncle Sam signing pledge "to Avoid Wars among the Americas" with Latin America. In background, uniformed "Europe" drinks gluttonously of "Overproof War Spirits" while impoverished "European Taxpayers" look hopelessly on.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The beginning of a campaign is depicted as a "fencing bout between gentlemen," with the Republican Party elephant and Democratic donkey respecting each other. The end of a campaign is "total war," with the two fighting and calling names.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A "Mud Thrower," covered in mud, tells the "whispering campaigner" to "go away - you're dirtier than I am."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wendell Willkie tries to debate with President Franklin Delano Roosevelt on domestic issues, past mistakes, and debts. Roosevelt, firing a gun representing "dramatic defense activities" cannot hear him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An undecided voter is wooed by several politicians.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam, switching from the Democrat Party donkey to the Republican elephant before crossing a stream representing war. A man representing the New Deal tells him "You shouldn't change in midstream," even though he has yet to enter the stream.

did.unittitle: "Found - A Man Who is Anxious to Work"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Woodrow Wilson tries to get out of bed, against Dr. Grayson's orders, because there is so much to do.

did.unittitle: "And spittin' out putty an' screeches an' glass, he starts a-shottin' from where he lights.",

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bald frontiersman with four-paned window around his neck aims rifle at figure disappearing into the background.

odd.head: General
odd.p: "Puck" january 23, 1909, 5 illustrations, The Power of Animals

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wizened figure behind a counter asks, "Vell, How Mooch D'ye Vont?" as young boy lopes toward him carrying a huge padlock.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: William Jennings Bryan, a critic of evolution, walks by the "family tree" and is hit on the head by a coconut thrown by a monkey.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam is struck by lightening from a storm representing Hoover "Prosperity."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man shoots another man to steal his pig, representing the five billion dollar liquor traffic.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Andrew Carnegie, dressed as a king, feasts on "$40,000,000 tax refund to Mellon Interests" while a Mellon employee receives a notice of a wage cut.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Democratic Party donkey and Republican Party elephant are both thin and sweating in the desert.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A weeping woman says goodbye to her son, a U.S. soldier.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Old man reading newspaper full of incidents of domestic unrest while wife in background says, "That must be Russia you're reading about, Abner. They're so lawless!" He responds, "Glad you think so, Mandy."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Huge silhouette of "Prussianism" nailed to fence of "Democracy" with "Liberty Bond" nails by common man singing "[His] Roll."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Logo of American Red Cross shining above the globe.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Club-carrying "Bolshevik" hurdles in the "Inski" side of the revolving door of "Russian government" as "Kerensky Forces" are booted out "Outski" side.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A drawing for the war effort (WWI) with the following quotation from President Wilson; "America is privileged to spend her blood and her might for the principles that gave her birth and happiness and the peace which she has treasured."

did.unittitle: "A Little More Rope and We'll Get Him!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An American soldier chases a German soldier back to Berlin, holding a noose to catch him. The noose is made up of the Liberty Loans used to fund U.S. efforts in World War I, tied together by Uncle Sam. A "volunteer bond buyer" brings rope from the fourth liberty loan so the American soldier can keep going.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: American soldiers in World War I are given more strength by the purchase of Liberty Bonds, allowing them to hit Prussianism harder.

did.unittitle: "The Good that May Spring from Evil."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A flower representing World Peace grows from a skull representing the European War (World War I).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A bald eagle, representing the U.S.A., attacks a shark representing submarine terrorism during World War I.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam is dragged into the waters of World War I by a shark representing U-Boat terrorism.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A figure representing "militarism" goes peacefully to his execution at the hands of a figure representing "the present European war" (World War I).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Benito Mussolini is chased from Ethiopia by John Bull, while Ethiopia's Emperor Haile Selassie laughs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Italian dictator Benito Mussolini dreams of being boiled in a pot by Ethiopians.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Adolf Hitler and Benito Mussolini eat together, both unhappy. Hitler eats a dog and Mussolini a black bird.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man representing the public catches Congress taking a "pension grab" from the U.S. Treasury. Now that he is caught, Congress considers putting it back.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wendell Willkie fishes for voters using "money issues," but the fish doesn't bite.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three men in a row boat representing Wendell Willkie's supporters: big money, the Republican party, and privilege. They are stuck on the rocks because the water level of "Willkie Popularity" has gone down so much.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A bowling ball, representing President Franklin Delano Roosevelt, knocks down all of the pins, representing the Republican Party campaign issues.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Republican elephant with a large cannon for the presidential campaign, but all he has for ammunition are small bullets representing Wendell Willkie wisecracks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Japanese Navy destroys Singapore's "impregnable defenses."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Transition through life regarding attitude toward Christmas.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Nassau Street scene, 3 cameos, the problems and inconveniences of life for a Princeton student.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Dated 1912. Title "The Princeton Student as described by the World's Book." is a crossed out.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Harvard-Princeton football game, many socially and racially interesting comments.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Dated 1912. Title "If we were to believe what lots of people say about us both." is crossed out.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Nine Cameos of spring time at Princeton.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Uses much archaic slang. Dated 1912. Title is crossed out.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mr. Milquetoast scared out of reading play to his wife by cautionary note on flyleaf.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam offers a few coins to a soldier as his bonus.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam as a doctor tries to cure a man from Presidential Ambitions in the "native son" ward.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Farley and McNutt get on their knees to listen to President Franklin Delano Roosevelt's announcement about the third term.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt overstays his welcome in the home of an elderly couple.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt turns away as if refusing a third term, but reaches out with this other hand to take a "third piece of pie" from a woman representing the Democratic Party.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Republican Party elephant rings the Liberty bell at the G.O.P. Convention in Philadelphia in 1940.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wendell Willkie is the bee that stung Ferdinand, who represents the Franklin Delano Roosevelt administration.

did.unittitle: "Is This the Place to Change Horses?"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt jumps from the horse "Democracy" to the horse "Dictator Policy," pulling Uncle Sam with him. Both horses are struggling to swim in deep water.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Hatch Bill is losing a boxing match with a large man representing politics. His gloves are "political spending" and "election year."

did.unittitle: "Of Course I Signed the Hatch Bill!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt tells the Republican Party elephant "Of course I signed the Hatch Bill," but the elephant only has a small amount of campaign funds due to the $5,000 individual limit and Roosevelt has a huge bag of funds from various organizations: TVA, NYA, WPA, AAA, and CCC.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt, dressed as Santa, tries to explain to a man how the guns and airplanes he needs are "on order" but not currently available.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt is hugged and smothered by a large woman representing the 3rd Term.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man leans on a shovel, sleeping, while holding a sign that reads "WPA Unfair to Workers!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Republican Party elephant as a hobo with a small bag of "campaign issues" looks up happily as "Nazi White Paper Charges" fall from the sky.

did.unittitle: "There Can Be No Dictator Where There are Free Elections -F.D.R."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt proclaims that there are free elections, while a man representing voters is tied to a ballot box by political machines, the "draft" gag, U.S. Treasure, and W.P.A. vote buying.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt defeats a man representing the "third term tradition" at cards, taking his money and his clothes. The cards spell out "New Deal".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several "bosses" stand outside the U.S. Treasury with a ballot box: Flynn, Lewis, Hague, Kelly, Guffey, and Nash.

did.unittitle: "Scram! Can't You See the cook has Neither the Time nor Inclination to Argue About it!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt as a cook, reading a book entitled "on hand or order book" and cooking "alphabet soup." Around him, debt, unemployment, and taxes pile up as dirty dishes, waste piles on the floor, and a cat representing bureaucrats drinks spilled milk.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Benito Mussolini writes Santa Claus asking him for spurs (German aid) to help in Albania and Egypt, and to hurry because Christmas will be too late.

did.unittitle: "Of Course This Couldn't Have Anything To Do with It!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Roosevelt announces plans to make five political speeches before the election as Wendell Willkie's popularity rises.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A figure representing the World, bandaged and in a wheel chair, writes an angry letter to Hitler in the form of a Valentine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Adolf Hitler rolls down a hill from Moscow, and a snowball begins to form around him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam writes his War Plan, which starts with war planes and pilots by the thousands.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three eccentric characters in foreground discussing prize-winning produce. Background in chaos as people of all shapes and sizes enjoy a day at the fair. Unfinished old man on reverse.

odd.head: General
odd.p: "Puck," August 14, 1912. Unfinished old man on reverse.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portly man speaking with elder man on main street of very rural town.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Admiral Bell.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Democratic Party donkey and Republican elephant both shout on the telephone to an independent voter during a political campaign.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Franklin Delano Roosevelt sits in a smoke-filled room talking via a private wire to Chicago, where the Democratic National Convention was held in 1940.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A submarine representing war sinks a ship representing world commerce.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Benito Mussolini is startled as he walks passed a statue of Caesar that says "humph."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Adolf Hitler and Benito Mussolini try to intimidate the United States by shouting "Boo!" Uncle Sam ignores them and prepares aid for Britain.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Front-page cartoon in October 1873 edition of the New York Daily Graphic. IN style peculiar to Wust called Graphic Statues.

odd.head: General
odd.p: "A History of American Graphic Humor 1865-1938" by William Murrell. Inscription on reverse.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A Nazi soldier and a collaborator walk through the streets of Paris as the Allies advance in the distance. A woman, representing the French underground, prepares to attack them.

did.unittitle: "Destroying the Statue of George II at the Bowling Green New York",

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two in one cartoon with cameo in lower left titled "Destroying the Statue of George III at the Bowling Green New York" super-imposed on skyline populated by buildings with royal appellations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dining room scene with fashionable ladies and gentlemen.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Shows "Chairman Clapp" of GOP entering office with hat extended only to have everyone hide.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Uncle Sam encourages Woodrow Wilson to keep firing notes at Europe from a cannon of diplomacy, telling him they have made some good hits.

did.unittitle: "It works swell! I get in my trap drumming practice and spring cleaning at the same time!"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man has attached his drum set to a carpet beater so he can play and clean the carpet at the same time.

did.unittitle: "Don't shoot, Clara, said Desmond quickly I shall tell you all"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A woman points a gun at a man.

did.unittitle: "Quick Watson the needle muttered Holmes in a whisper?"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sherlock Holmes examines a piece of paper with a magnifying glass while Dr. Watson watches.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A Harvard man, Fred, orders two sailors to fire a cannon.

did.unittitle: "Paul the Peddler or Bound to Win"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two men talking.

did.unittitle: "The Question is not Will It Win but Is It Right"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of Woodrow Wilson.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An eagle flies down to help a burning Manila.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: President Woodrow Wilson looking across the ocean to a pack of barking dogs labeled "jingoes."

did.unittitle: "Drink - for Once Dead You Never Shall Return"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A man encourages another, who is eating a free lunch, to drink.

did.unittitle: "Gosh! A Fellow Can Dream Can't He?"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A soldier at the front, a worker in a plant, and a worker in an office all dream of finishing with their dog.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A woman looks at two crying children in a waiting room.
